lith_description: OVERBURDEN
TOP OF BEDROCK REACHED AT 2M, NO RECOVERY UNTIL 6M

MED-LIGHT GREY DIORITE. SOME SUB-ROUNDED CLASTS (VOLCANOCLASTIC?) IN SPOTS UP TO 6CM. GROUNDMASS IS MOSTLY FINE-MED GRAINED, SUBVOLCANIC LOOKING WITH POSSIBLE QUENCHING ZONES THAT LOOK TO HAVE SOME LAYERING/FLOW BANDING. SMALL PLAG PHENOS AVG 1MM AND ACTINOLITE/CHLORITE ALTERED ACICULAR HORNBLENDE PHENOS AVG 1.5MM THROUGHOUT. WK-STR MAGNETIC WITH TINY BLEBS OF MAGNETITE THROUGHOUT.

LIGHT GREEN-GREY AND MED RED VOLCANOCLASTIC DIRORITE. CHAOTIC VOLCANOCLASTIC/BRECCIATED LOOK TO THE ROCK, MANY FRAGMENTS WITH SOME LIGHT GREY-GREEN COLOURED AND OTHERS RED HEM STAINED. RED HEM STAINED ROCK MIGHT BE A MATRIX OF SOME SORT, COULD BE RHYOLITE IN ORIGIN. SOME ALTERED PALG AND HORNBLENDE TEXTURE SLIGHTLY VISIBLE, UP TO 2MM PHENOS WHERE VISIBLE. CLASTS IN ROCK ARE AROUND A COUPLE CMS AVERAGE. SOME PATCHES OF WK MAG.

LIGHT-DARK GREEN DIORITE. HIGHLY ALTERED DIORITE WITH MAGNETITE AND EP, POSSIBLY SUB-VOLCANIC, POSSIBLE VOLCANOCLASTIC. SOME VISIBLE PHENOS AVG 1MM OF PLAG AND HORNBLENDE. A SMALL ZONE OF WHAT COULD BE A DYKLET OR A XENOLITH OF THE RED HEM STAINED ROCKTYPE SEEN ABOVE WITH CALCITE VEINING. HIGHLY MAGNETIC THROUGHOUT.

MED GREY AND RED VOLCANOCLASTIC/BRECCIATED DIORITE. SIMILAR TO THE UNIT ABOVE THE MAGNETITE/EPIDOTE ALTERED UNIT. THE RED POSSIBLY RHYOLITE ROCK SEEMS TO HAVE SOME ZONES WHERE THEY ARE ALL GOING IN THE SAME DIECTION LIKE LAYERING, ~45 DTCA, COULD BE DUE TO PRESSURE INJECTING THE FLUID THROUGH THE DIORITE. TOP CONTACT LOOKS TO BE ~28 DTCA. ALT LOOKS MORE PHYLLIC THAN BEFORE, OTHER THAN THE RED HEM. MOSTLY NO-MAG TO WK MAG WITH SOME MODERATE PATCHES.

INTENSLY ALTERED AND FAULTED GREEN-BLACK DIORITE. UNIT STARTS OFF LOOKING SIMILAR TO THE EP/MAG ALTERED DIORITE ABOVE BUT BECOMES EVEN MORE INTENSELY ALTERED TO GREEN AND BLACK FROM 29-35 WITH SOME FAULTED ZONES OF RUBBLE AND GOUGE. SOME TEXTURE IS PRESEREVED SHOWING FINE-MED GRAINED DIORITE WITH SMALL 1MM PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS.
CORE LOSS IN FAULTY MATERIAL, DRILLER HAD TO RE-COLLAR TO 57M, ONLY ~2M RECOVERED FROM 44-57M WHICH IS PROBABLY THE LAST BIT OF ROCK PULLED UP.

ZONE OF HEAVILY FAULTED ANDESITE AND POSSIBLE DIORITE, MED GREY WITH SOME LIGHT GREEN. HIGHLY FAULTED AND COULD BE ANDESITE WITH FRAGMENTS/BX OF DIORITE-MICRODIORITE. MOD-STR MAG THROUGHOUT IN PATCHES.
MED GREY FINE GRAINED ANDESITE DYKE. SOME SPARCE CAL AND EP FILLED AMYGDULES. WK-MOD MAG.
SAME AS ABOVE THE ANDESITE DYKE.

MED GREY FINE GRAINED ANDESITE DYKE. MOSTLY FINE GRAINED WITH SOME SPOTTY ACT ALTERED HORNBLENDE MACULES OCCASIONALLY SEEN (MELANOCRATIC). SOME WHITE SPOTS OF CALCITE THAT COULD BE SPARCE AMYGDULES. WK-MOD MAG.

MED GREY DIORITE WITH SOME BRECCIATED AND SUBVOLCANIC. PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS THROUGHOUT UP TO 2MM, HORNBLENDE LOOKING USUALLY SMALLER THAN PLAG. LOOKS LESS FAULTED THAN FAULTING ABOVE ANDESITE BUT STILL VERY FRACTURED/BROKEN. SOME SECTIONS WITH STRONG BRECCIATION, DIORITE CLASTS FLOATING IN SLIGHTLY FINER GRAINED MATRIX. SOME ZONES THAT LOOK TO HAVE FLOW BANDING AND/OR DEFORMATION CAUSING UNDULATORY LAYERS. WK PCHY MAG OCCASIONALLY WITH SOME SLIGHTLY MOD MAG. SOME SPOTS WITH A TINY ORANGE/BROWN MINERAL THAT ISN'T VERY HARD, HAS A SLIGHT CLEAVAGE, POSSIBLY ELONGATE SPHENE SHAPE, DOTTED IN THE GROUNDMASS, POSSIBLE TITANITE. THERE APPEARS TO BE A SMALL 25CM ANDESITE DYKLET WITH AMYGDULES AT 94.65-94,90M THAT LOOKS JUST LIKE THE OTHER ANDESITE DYKES ENCOUNTERED IN THE HOLE.

MED-DARK GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE GRAINED WITH SOME ACT/CHL ALTERED HORNBLENDE PHENOS UP TO 3MM. CHILL MARGINS ON THE TOP AND BOTTOM OF UNIT WITH NO PHENOS AND FINER GROUNDMASS. SMALL ~1MM CALCITE FILLED AMYGDULES THROUGHOUT. MOD MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT. BOTTOM CONTACT IS APPROX 45 DTCA.

MED-DARK GREY-GREEN DIORITE. HEAVILY FRACTURED AND FAULTED, SOME THINGS HARD TO SEE IN ROCK. LOOKS SLIGHTLY VOLCANOCLASTIC WITH SOME SUB-ROUNDED CLASTS IN GROUNDMASS BUT NOT ENOUGH TO BE BRECCIA. FINE-MED GRAINED, PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS UP TO ~2MM IN SIZE. STRONG PROP ALT SEEMS TO BE MAKING ROCK SOFT AND BRITTLE THROUGHOUT. MOD-STR PATCHES OF MAG THROUGHOUT WITH SOME UN-MAGNETIC ROCK. THERE IS ONE SMALL ANDESITE DYKLET AT ~137.10M (DIFFICULT TO TELL WHERE IT STARTS OR END IN THE BROKEN ROCK), ~15CM LONG. LAST ~1M HAS MAGNETITE AND EPIDOTE IN SOME OF THE FAULT RUBBLE
DARK GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE GRAINED WITH SOME ACT ALTERED HORNBLENDE PHENOS UP TO 4MM. CHILLED MARGINS AT TOP AND BOTTOM. CAL FILLED AMYGDULES.

BLACK MAGNETITE SKARN ALTERED ROCK WITH SOME GREEN. ROCK HAS BEEN COMPLETELY ALTERED AND REPLACED WITH MOSTLY MAGNETITE AND SOME EPIDOTE. SOME PATCHES OF PROP MINERALS CAN BE SEEN BUT ALL TEXTURE HAS BEEN DESTROYED AND THE ROCK IS MOSTLY SANDY. THE ROCK IS RUBBLY FROM FAULTING PROBABLY. SOME LARGE VNS AND BLEBS OF CPY THROUGHOUT. SHARP TOP CONTACT BETWEEN ANDESITE AND SKARN IS INTACT, 39 DTCA.

LIGHT-MED GREY-GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC DIORITE. VARIEGATED COLOUR BETWEEN GREY AND GREEN EP. MUCH OF ROCK IS BROKEN UP AND FLTED. CHAOTIC LOOKING BRECCIATION IN INTACT PIECES, MANY LITH TYPES, DIORITE PIECES WITH SOME ANDESITE, POSSIBLE RHYOLITE AND/OR MONZONITE, FRAGS OF THE MAGNETITE SKARN LOOKING MATERIAL, MANY SUB-ROUNDED WITH A FEW SLIGHTLY ANGULAR, AVG SIZE IS A FEW CMS. MUCH OF TEXTURE IS OBSCURED BY ALT AND RUBBLE, SOME PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS VISIBLE UP TO 2MM. PATCHY WK-MOD MAG THROUGHOUT, SOME SPOTS WITH NO MAG. A SMALL ANDESITE DYKE IS FOUND AT 219.70-219.92M THAT LOOKS LIKE THE OTHERS PREVIOUSLY SEEN.

MED-DARK GREY-GREEN TUFFACEOUS TO VOLCANIC ANDESITE. THIS UNIT LOOKS LIKE IT COULD BE TWO TYPES OF VOLCANIC ROCK OR DYKES; THE TOP FROM 227.5-229M IS UNUSUALLY LOW IN SIO2 (FROM XRF, ~40%) WHICH MAKES IT NEARLY BASALTIC IN COMPOSITION. THE TOP OF THE UNIT ALSO DISPLAYS WHAT LOOKS LIKE TUFFACEOUS TEXTURES LIKE FLOW BANDING/DEFORMATION WITH GLASSY WHITE TABULAR CLASTS OR PHENOS (PLAG?) UP TO 4MM AND EUTAXTIC VEINING (UNDULATORY/DEFORMED VEINING AROUND THE CLASTS/CRYSTALS) BUT IT ALSO HAS SIMILAR PORPHYRITIC HORNBLENDE THROUGHOUT LIKE THE ONE BELOW OR THE DYKES SEEN EARLIER IN THE HOLE. THE GROUNDMASS IS FINE WITH CHL/ACT ALT THROUGHOUT AND MANY SMALL BLEBS OF HEMATITE-MAGNETITE. TOP AND BOTTOM OF THIS SECTION IS BROKEN UP AND MIGHT HAVE CORE LOSS SO IT IS UNCLEAR IF THERE COULD BE CHILL MARGINS. FROM 229~230.85M MAY BE INCORPORATED ROCK FROM THE DIORITE OR MORE MESSED UP VOLCANICS/DYKE MATERIAL BUT IT'S UNCLEAR DUE TO FLTING AND RUBBLE. FROM ~230.85-233.26M THE ROCK LOOKS MUCH MORE LIKE THE ANDESITE DYKES SEEN BEFORE WITH PORPHYRITIC HORNBLENDE REPLACED BY HORNBLENDE AND CONTAINING CAL AMYGDULES. THERE ARE CHILL MARGINS AT THE START AND END OF THIS "TRUE" ANDESITE. WK-MOD MAG THROUGHOUT EXCEPT THROUGH THE MIDDLE SECTION WHERE THERE IS TRACE-WK MAGNETISM.
MED GREY-DARK GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC/BRECCIATED DIORITE. SIMILAR TO THE UNIT ABOVE THE DYKE WITH POLYMICTIC CLASTS/BRECCIA COMPOSITION. WK MAG THROUGHOUT WITH SOME MOD PATCHES.

DARK GREEN TO BLACK SKARN ALTERED ROCK. SOME SPOTS HAVE SLIGHTLY LESS SKARN ALT AND SEEM TO LOOK LIKE A DIORITE LIKE ABOVE, SO IS PROBABLY THE PROTOLITH. ROCK IS MOSTLY JUST FINE GRAINED ACTINOLITE AND MAGNETITE WITH EP AND SPOTS OF BROWN-RED GARNETS (ANDRADITE?) THAT LOOK SLIGHTLY DECOMPOSED. THE DIORITE LOOKING SECTIONS HAVE SOME MED GRAINED CRYSTALS VISIBLE. MOD-INTENSE MAGNETISM, ESPECIALLY IN THE BLACK MAGNETITE SECTION.

LIGHT GREEN-GREY DIORITE. POSSIBLY BRECCIATED, HARD TO SEE ANY TEXTURE IN INTENSE PROPYLITIC ALT AND MOST IS BROKEN INTO SMALL PIECES. ROCK IS COMPOSED MOSTLY OF WHAT LOOKS LIKE CHLORITE, SERICITE, AND CALCITE GROUNDMASS WITH DARK GREEN SPOTS THAT ARE PROBABLY ACTINOLITE REPLACING HORNBLENDE THAT LOOK UP TO 3MM IN SIZE. NO MAGNETISM.

BLACK TO DARK GREEN MAGNETITE SKARN. ALMOST COMPLETELY COMPOSED OF MAGNETITE AND SOME ACTINOLITE. PROTOLITH COULD BE FAULTED VOLCANICS DUE TO HOW RUBBLY AND GOUGY THE ROCK LOOKS, AND THE BOTTOM LOOKS LIKE GOUGE FAULTS. TEXTURE IS LIKE A SILTY CLAY WITH SANDY MATERIAL

LIGHT GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC/BRECCIATED DIORITE. MOST OF UNIT IS RUBBLE SO TEXTURES AND FEATURES ARE HARD TO SEE. SOME INTACT ZONES HAVE CLASTS OF POSSIBLY DIORITE AND OTHER ROCK TYPES UP TO 4CM. GROUNDMASS IS QUITE CARBONACEOUS, MOSTLY COMPOSED OF WHAT LOOKS LIKE CHL CAL SER AND ACT. POSSIBLE PHENOS OF HORNBLENDE THAT HAVE BEEN REPLACED BY SER AND/OR CAL UP TO 2MM. WK PCHY MAG THROUGHOUT.

MED GREY-GREEN ANDESITE DYKE. SIMILAR TO THE OTHER ANDESITE DYKES SEEN THROUGHOUT THE HOLE. PORPHYRITIC 2MM HORNBLENDE REPLACED BY ACTINOLITE, LESS AND SMALLER CAL FILLED AMYGDULES. MOD MAG, BUT NO BIOTITE THIS TIME. LESS PHENOS AND FINER GRAINED AT TOP AND BOTTOM, CHILL MARGIN.

MED GREEN-GREY BECCIATED DIORITE. CHAOTIC LOOKING TEXTURE WITH SOME DEFORMATION. SOME PORPH TEXTURE SEEN IN SOME ROCK WITH MANY CLASTS OF POSSIBLY ANDESITE/DIORITE ORIGIN. SOME LOOKS TO HAVE LAYERING FROM EITHER DEFORMATION METAMORPHISM OR FLOW BANDING. OF PHENOS SEEN IT LOOKS LIKE ALTERED PLAG AND HORNBLENDE ~2MM IN SIZE. WK-MOD PCHY MAG THROUGHOUT.

MED GREEN-GREY ANDESITE DYKE. LOOKS SIMILAR TO THE DYKES SEEN THROUGHOUT THE HOLE, EXCEPT THE BLACK SPOTS FROM ABOUT THE MIDDLE AND DOWN ARE LARGER UP TO 8MM AND LOOK MORE LIKE CLASTS. THESE "CLASTS" LOOK SUB-ROUNDED TO ANGULAR AND ARE SLIGHTLY MOTTLED BLACK AND WHITE, WHICH COULD BE ACT AND SER OR CHL REPLACING THEM (DARKER ON THE OUTSIDE AND MORE TRANSLUSCENT-GREEN TOWARDS THE INSIDE). MOD MAG THROUGHOUT. NO APPARENT CHILL MARGINS. SLIGHTLY AMYDALOIDAL WITH CAL FILL.

LIGHT-MED GREY-GREEN BRECCIATED DIORITE. PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS ARE VISIBLE, MOSTLY 1MM IN SIZE. CHAOTIC LOOKING BRECCIA CLASTS SEEN THROUGHOUT UP TO A FEW CMS, COMPOSED OF DIORITE AND POSSIBLY ANDESITE. THERE IS WHAT LOOKS LIKE A SMALL ANDESITE DYKE FROM ~290.35-290.75M THAT LOOKS SIMILAR TO THE OTHER DYKES THAT HAVE BEEN SEEN THROUGH THE HOLE. PAST THIS DYKE THE ROCK BECOMES VERY BROKEN AND FAULTED UNTIL ~318M.

BLACK TO DARK GREEN MAGNETITE SKARN. UNIT IS ALMOST COMPLETELY ALTERED TO MAGNETITE SKARN RICH IN ACT MAG AND PY. LOTS OF THE UNIT HAS CLAY AND CRUMBLY ROCK SO MAY HAVE BEEN A FLT OR SERIES OF FLTS. GRAINSIZE IS FROM A FINE CLAY LIKE MAG DUST TO MED GRAINED CRYSTALS. HIGHLY MAGNETIC.

MED-PALE GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. ROCK IS CHAOTICALLY FILLED WITH MANY TYPED OF LITH CLASTS THAT ARE MOSTLY SUB-ROUNDED AND HAVING COMPOSITIONS THAT COULD POSSIBLY INCLUDE ANDESITE, DIORITE, RHYOLITE, MONZONITE, AND MAYBE OTHERS. AN XRF SCAN ON SOME BLACK AND LIGHT PINK CLASTS SHOWED THAT EVEN THOUGH THE CLASTS LOOK TO HAVE DIFFERENT MAFIC-TO-FELSIC COMPOSITIONS DUE TO THEIR COLOUR, THEY HAVE VERY HIGH SIO2 CONTENT OF AROUND 80% WHILE HAVING LOW FE AND MG WHICH COULD BE DUE TO SILICIFICATION OF THE ENTIRE UNIT. CLASTS RANGE IN SIZE FROM LESS THAN A CM TO 10 OR MORE CMS. THE MATRIX IS FINE GRAINED ANDESITE. THERE ARE A FEW SMALL SECTIONS THAT SEEM TO HAVE A LOWER CLAST AMOUNT WHICH COULD BE A LULL IN VOLCANIC ACTIVITY, AND ALSO SOME SMALL SECTIONS WHERE THE MATRIX LOOKS LIKE MICRODIORITE.

DARK GREY FINE-GRAINED ANDESITE DYKE. THIS DYKE LOOKS MUCH DIFFERENT THAN THE PREVIOUS DYKES SEEN EARLIER; IT HAS EPIDOTE SPOTS WHICH ARE PROBABLY AMYGDULES, NO PHENOS UNLESS THE EPIDOTE SPOTS ARE ALTERED PHENOS, AND SOME CHALCO BLEBS IN THE GROUNDMASS AND WITH THE EPIDOTE AMYGDULES. THIS DYKE MIGHT HAVE BEEN FORMED EARLIER OR RELATIVELY SYN-VOLCANIC. THERE DOESN'T APPEAR TO BE DEFINITE CHILL MARGINS AND THE CONTACT AT THE TOP DOESN'T LOOK VERY SHARP. MOD MAG THROUGHOUT.

LIGHT-MED GREY TO GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC BRECCIA. THIS UNIT IS THE SAME AS THE ONE ABOVE THE DYKE. AROUND ~362M THE ROCK STOP BEING SO BROKEN AND STARTS BEING MUCH MORE COMPETENT; THE BIGGEST CHANGE AROUND THIS AREA IS IT OCCURS RIGHT WHEN GYPSUM VNS START APPEARING SO COULD BE RELATED. SOME SECTIONS HAVE CLASTS THAT ARE QUITE ROUNDED AND PACKED, ALMOST LOOKS LIKE CONGLOMERATE.
MED GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE-GRAINED, AMYGDULES FILLED WITH CAL, SOME SMALL 1MM PHENOS OF ACT ALTERED HORNBLENDE. WK-MOD MAGNETIC

MED-DARK GREY TO GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC BRECCIA. THIS UNIT IS THE SAME AS THE ONE ABOVE THE DYKE. SOME SECTIONS ONCE AGAIN HAVE CLASTS THAT ARE QUITE ROUNDED AND PACKED, ALMOST LOOKS LIKE CONGLOMERATE. THERE IS ANOTHER SMALL DYKE THAT LOOKS IDENTICAL TO THE ONE ABOVE THIS UNIT AT 395.7-396.05CM.

MED GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE-GRAINED, CHILL MARGINS AND SOME CLASTS INCORPORATED AT THE EDGES. THE CLASTS HAVE A DISTORTED/LAYERED LOOKING CHILL HALO. ~2MM SIZE PHENOS OF HORNBLENDE ARE ALTERED BY ACT AND HEM. WK-MOD MAGNETISM.
MED-DARK GREY-GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. SAME ROCK UNIT AS ABOVE THE ANDESITE DYKE. THERE SEEM TO BE MORE ALTERNATING LIGHT AND DARK PATCHES GOING DOWN SUGGESTING POSSIBLE BI-MODAL VOLCANISM.

MED GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE GRAINED, AMYDULES FILLED WITH CAL AND SOME WITH HEM BLEBS, CHILL MARGINS AT THE TOP AND BOTTOM. SOME DARKER SPOTS THAT ARE PROBABLY HORNBLENDE PHENOS THAT HAVE BEEN ALTERED WITH ACT AND HEM, LOOK POORLY FORMED. BOTTOM CONTACT IS AT 44 DTCA
MED-DARK GREY-GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. SAME ROCK UNIT AS ABOVE THE ANDESITE DYKE. THERE ARE A COUPLE SMALL ANDESITE DYKLETS UP TO 4CM WIDE CUTIING THROUGH.
MED GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE GRAINED AND CAL FILLED AMYGDULES. NO PHENOS IN THIS DYKE, UNLIKE THE OTHERS. CHILLED MARGINS. WK-MOD MAGNETISM.

MED-DARK GREY-GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. SAME ROCK UNIT AS ABOVE THE ANDESITE DYKE. THERE IS A SECTION WHERE THERE ARE NO CLASTS IN THE ROCK AND THE GRAINSIZE STARTS ALTERNATING BETWEEN FINE AND MEDIUM GRAINED FROM 483.03-489.67M. THIS SECTION DOESN'T LOOK LIKE A TYPICAL DYKE AS IT LACKS CHILL MARGINS, THE CONTACTS SEEM MORE GRADATIONAL, AND THE GRAINSIZE CHANGES THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL, SO IS PROBABLY A SYN-SUBVOLCANIC PHASE OF THE SAME UNIT. BOTTOM OF UNIT IS STILL VOLCANOCLASTIC BUT SEEMS TO GRADATIONALLY HAVE LESS CLASTS IN THE LAST COUPLE METRES

MED GREY AND GREEN SPOTTED PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE. FINE-MED GRAINED WITH A FINE GROUNDMASS AND MED GRAINED PHENOS OF HORNBLENDE UP TO 3MM IN SIZE. EP IS SPECKLED THROUGHOUT GROUNDMASS, POSSIBLY MANY REPLACING PHENOS. SMALL POSSIBLE ANDESITE DYKE CUTTING THROUGH THE ROCK AT 525.96-526.68M THAT IS DARKER AND FINE GRAINED WITH SMALLER PHENOS, COULD BE SYN-VOLCANIC.

MED-DARK GREY AND RED VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. LOOKS SIMILAR TO THE OTHER VOLCANOCLASTICS ABOVE; SUB-ROUNDED TO ROUNDED CLASTS THAT LOOK LIKE CONGLOMERATE IN A FINE GRAINED ANDESITE MATRIX, POLYMICTIC CLASTS. MANY CLASTS ARE REDDISH FROM HEM ALT AND/OR MAY HAVE POT ALT.
DARK GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE GRAINED, NO OBVIOUS CHILL MARGINS. SOME SMALL HORNBLENDE PHENOS IN GROUNDMASS UP TO 1MM.
MED GREY TO RED VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. SAME AS THE ROCK TYPE ABOVE THE DYKE.
LIGHT GREY-RED ANDESITE DYKE. FINE GRAINED, PORPHYRITIC, CAL FILLED AMYGDULES. ONE LARGE XENOLITH 5CM IN SIZE. CHILLED MARGING AT THE EDGES. BOTTOM CONTACT IS AT 39 DTCA
SAME VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE AS ABOVE DYKE, THE COLOUR IS MORE RED FROM HEM ALT. ANOTHER SMALL DYKLET THAT LOOKS RELATIVELY THE SAME AS THE ONE ABOVE IS GOING THROUGH THE UNIT AT 592.11-592.8M.
MED-DARK GREY ANDESITE DYKE. UP TO 2MM HORNBLENDE PHENOS, CAL FILLED AMYGDULES, NO OBVIOUS CHILL MARGINS.
MED-DARK GREY TO MAROON RED VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. SAME VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE AS BEFORE WITH MANY ROUNDED CLASTS AND A FINE-MED GRAINED ANDESITE MATRIX.

MED-DARK GREY ANDESITE. ZONES OF FINE AND MED GRAINED CRYSTALS ARE PRESENT, PROBABLY DUE TO MULTIPHASIC VOLCANIC EVENTS. SOME PHENOS HAVE A TRACHYTIC ALIGNMENT. ~2MM ACICULAR HORNBLENDE PHENOS AND SOME SMALLER PLAG PHENOS OCCASSIONALLY SEEN. RARE SUB-ROUNDED CLASTS SEEN UP TO 4CM IN SIZE. TOP CONTACT LOOKS LIKE IT'S RELATIVELY SHARP WITH POSSIBLE SHEARING ABOVE IT IN THE PREVIOUS UNIT.
DARK-MED GREY GREEN AND RED VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. THIS UNIT IS PRACTICALLY IDENTICAL TO THE VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE THAT OCCURRED ABOVE THE ANDESITE.

MED-DARK GREY ANDESITE WITH GREEN SPOTS OF EPIDOTE THROUGHOUT. GRAIN SIZE ALTERNATES THROUGHOUT WITH SOME FINE GRAINED AND SOME MED GRAINED. SOME SECTIONS WHERE PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS ARE UP TO 2MM IN SIZE. SOME SECTIONS HAVE LOTS OF EPIDOTE SPOTTED THROUGHOUT, POSSIBLY REPLACING THE PHENOS THAT WERE THERE. AT ~709-716M THERE ARE VOLCANOCLASTICS THAT LOOK LIKE THE VOLCANOCLASTIC UNIT ABOVE THAT COME IN AND OUT SLIGHTLY, STILL LOOKS MOSTLY LIKE ANDESITE AND IS PROBABLY A SMALL EVENT INBETWEEN ERUPTIONS.
DARK-MED GREY GREEN AND RED VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. THIS UNIT IS PRACTICALLY IDENTICAL TO THE VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE THAT OCCURRED ABOVE THE ANDESITE ONCE AGAIN.
MED GREY ANDESITE DYKE. FINE GRAINED, CHILL MARGINS AT THE EDGES, NO VISIBLE PHENOS, NO VISIBLE AMYGDULES. TOP CONTACT IS 38 DTCA
DARK-MED GREY GREEN AND RED VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. THIS UNIT IS PRACTICALLY IDENTICAL TO THE VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE THAT OCCURRED ABOVE THE ANDESITE DYKE ONCE AGAIN. EOH AT 732M
OVERBURDEN, NO CORE RECOVERED
BEDROCK HIT AT 2M, NO CORE RECOVERED

MED-DARK GREY VOLCANIC BRECCIA ANDESITE. SOME LARGE CLASTS IN ANDESITE MATRIX UP TO 8CM. ANDESITE IS FINE-MED GRAINED, SOME SECTIONS THAT LOOK LIKE MICRO DIORITE WITH PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS VISIBLE UP TO ~2MM. SOME FLTING AND BROKEN ROCK MAKING IT DIFFICULT TO SEE TEXTURES IN THE ROCK. WK-MOD MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT IN PATCHES

LIGHT-DARK GREEN TO BLACK SKARN. PROTOLITH LOOKS TO BE POSSIBLY A VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE OR DIORITE, MOST TEXTURE HAVE BEEN REPLACED BY SKARN MINERALS. SOME SHAPES THAT LOOK LIKE SUB-ROUNDED CLASTS OCCASSIONALY SEEN. SOME SECTIONS WITH LOTS OF GARNETS REPLACING MOST OF THE ORIGINAL ROCK

LIGHT-MED GREY AND GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE. FINE-MED GRAINED THROUGHOUT WITH SOME SPOTS THAT LOOK LIKE DIORITE AND OTHERS THAT LOOK LIKE ANDESITE, COULD BE DUE TO CLASTS. SOME PHENOS OF HORNBLENDE AND POSSIBLY PLAG SEEN, ALTERATION AND BROKEN UP ROCK OBSCURING MOST FEATURES.

MED-DARK GREEN TO GREY DIORITE. THIS UNIT STARTS OUT FAIRLY SKARN ALTERED SO THE TEXTURE IS HARD TO DISTINGUISH, BUT A FINE-MED GRAINED GROUNDMASS IS VISIBLE WITH WHAT LOOKS LIKE PHENOS. SOME PLAG AND HORNBLENDE PHENOS UP TO ~2MM OBSERVED. SOME OF THIS UNIT HAS FAULTING AND SHEARING WHICH MAKES THE TEXTURE HARDER TO DISTINGUISH AND SOME LOOKS TO BE DEFORMED BY SHEARING. SOME OF WHAT LOOKS LIKE CLASTS NEAR FAULTED ZONES BUT EP/SKARN/PHYLLIC ALTERATION IS MAKING THEM DIFFICULT TO DISTINGUISH.
CORE WAS LOST DUE TO DRILLERS NEEDING TO TRICONE THROUGH AND ADVANCE CASING TO 105M

MED-DARK GREEN TO GREY DIORITE. SAME TYPE OF DIORITE AS ABOVE THE LOST CORE INTERVAL. SOME OF THIS UNIT HAS FAULTING AND SHEARING WHICH MAKES THE TEXTURE HARDER TO DISTINGUISH AND SOME LOOKS TO BE DEFORMED BY SHEARING. SOME OF WHAT LOOKS LIKE CLASTS NEAR FAULTED ZONES BUT EP/SKARN/PHYLLIC ALTERATION IS MAKING THEM DIFFICULT TO DISTINGUISH. THE BOTTOM OF THIS UNIT IS QUITE ARGILLICALLY ALTERED SO TEXTURES ARE HARD TO DISTINGUISH, WITH THE CORE LOSS AT THE BOTTOM THIS MAY BE DUE TO A STRONG FAULT THAT WASN'T RECOVERED AND IS PROBABLY THE END TO UNIT AS SOME OF THE INTACT ROCK STILL SLIGHTLY LOOKS LIKE DIORITE.
DRILLER ENCOUNTERED PROBLEMS WITH DRILLING AND HAD TO TRICONE THROUGH THIS SECTION, NO CORE RECOVERED.

MED-DARK GEY-GREEN VOLCANOCLASTIC OR BRECCIATED ANDESITE. TOP CONTACT IS UNCLEAR AS THERE IS STRONG ARGILLIC ALT BEFORE CORE LOSS BUT AFTER 135.45 THERE ARE OBVIOUS CLASTS SEEN. MATRIX IS FINE-MED GRAINED WITH MOSTLY SUB-ANGULAR CLASTS COMPOSED OF POSSIBLE DIORITE AND ANDESITE. CLASTS RANGE FROM LESS THAN A CM TO MANY CMS. SLIGHTLY MAGNETIC IN SPOTS.

SKARN STARTS GRADUALLY WITH VARYING AMOUNTS OF MAGNETITE+PYRITE+EPIDOTE+CHALCOPYRITE+GARNET+ACTINOLITE. WHERE THE ALTERATION INSTENSITY IS LOW, PROTOLITH IS STILL THE SAME VOLCANIC BRECCIA. STRONGEST SKARN SECTION IS BETWEEN 185.5-194.5 M CHARACTERIZED WITH VERY FINE GRAINED MAGNETITE+PYRITE+EPIDOTE. SERICITE OVERPRINT THAT GRADES INTO CLAY ALTERATION IN SOME INTERVALS. POSSIBLE CAVE-IN MATERIAL BETWEEN 182-185.42 M. DOMINANT VEINING IS MAGNETITE+PY+CHALCOPYRITE CRACKLES AND VEINS. MINOR PY+EP+CP VEINS. PYRITE AND CHALCOPYRITE IS MOST LIKELY SECONDARY, REPLACING MAGNETITE SKARN AND MAGNETITE VEINS. LATE CALCITE VEINING.

SECTION STARTS WITH A 10 CM CALCITE VEIN WITH MINOR CPY+PY. THIS IS A FAULTED SECTION WITH INTENSE RUBBLE, FAULT GOUGE, AND CLAY ALTERED SECTION. ORIGINAL ROCK TEXTURE IS COMPLETELY ENHANCED AND THE PHOTOLITH IS ASSUMED TO BE CONTINUATION OF UPHOLE VOLCANIC BRECCIA UNIT. ORIGINAL EPIDOTE ALTERATION OVERPRINTED BY STRONG RETROGRADE CLAY ALTERATION. NO MAGNETITE (POSSIBLY DESTROYED AS A RESULT OF ALTERATION), AND MOST OF THE SULPHIDE MINERALIZATION IS LOST TO THE CLAY ALTERATION. ~0.5 CPY

MAGNETITE+EPIDOTE SKARN AFTER THE FAULT. COMPARED TO THE OTHER SKARN LEVEL, THIS SKARN IS CHARACTERIZED WITH MAGNETITE AND EPIDOTE WITH LESS SULPHIDES. VERY FINE GRAINED MAGNETITE AND EPIDOTE BANDS. POSSIBLY THIS SECTION IS NOT OVERPRINTED BY A SULPHIDE RICH FLUID. CLAY OVERPRINT. VERY LOW RECOVERY BUT LESS RUBBLE.

POLYMICTIC CONGLOMERATE WITH TUFF LAND PORPHYRY ANDESITE EVELS.WEAK TO MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALT DOMINATED WITH EP, MINOR CHL AND ACT. PROPYLITIC ALT IS MOSTLY CONFINED IN XENOLITHS OR PROPYLITIC VEINS. PROPYLITIC IS OVERPRINTED BY SERICITE AND SILICIFICATION. CLAY ALTERATION IS DOMINANT BETWEEN 217.5-230 M AND 241-251.42 M. GROUNDMASS AND XENNOLITHS HAVE A VERY DULL, GLASSY LOOK DUE TO THE SILICIFICATION. DOMINANT VEINING IS LATE CALCITE AND TYPE 2 PROPYLITIC+/-CALCITE VEINS. THESE VEINS USUALLY CONTAIN CLAY MINERALS AND LEACHED OUT WITH THE CLAY ALTERATION. GENERALLY, POOR IN SULPHIDES EXCEPT A FEW INTERVALS WITH CCP AND PY DUSTING (217.5-233 M). CRUSHED SECTION BETWEEN 241-251.42 M. SECTION ENDS WITH A CHILLED MARGIN (STRONG EPIDOTE+CLAY ALTERATION) RELATED TO THE DYKE CONTACT AT 251.42 M.

WEST ZONE POST-MINERAL MICRODIORITE/ANDESITE DYKE. SECTION INCLUDES HORSES OF COUNTRY ROCK BETWEEN 258-259 M. WEAK CHL ALTERATION OF HORNBLENDE PHEONCRYSTS, AND A WEAK SERICITE OVERPRINT. NO SULPHIDES. LATE CALCITE VEINING.

SOME SECTIONS RESEMBLE A DIORITE, SOME ARE VOLCANIC BRECCIA BUT TOO RUBBLY TO MAKE CONCLUSION. VOLCANIC BRECCIA WITH SYN-VOLCANIC INTRUSIONS. EP+CHL ALT OVERPRINTED BY CLAY AND SERICITE FOCUSED MOSTLY ON FRACTURES. DIS CCP AND PY COMMON BUT IRREGULARLY DISTRIBUTED. DOMINANT VEINING IS TYPE 3B (EP+PY), PARTIALLY DESTROYED BY CLAY ALTERATION.

SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE. NO MORE DIORITE AND THE CORE RESEMBLES VOLCANIC BRECCIA. SIMILAR ALTERATION AND VEINING TYPES. PROPYLITIC OVERPTINTED BY CLAY. LESS DIS CCP AND PY.
WEST ZONE POST-MINERAL GREEN AMYGDULE ANDESITE DYKE. AMYGDULES ARE FILLED WITH CALCITE AND MINOR EPIDOTE. VERY FINE ACTINOLITE RICH GROUNDMASS. TYPE 2 CAL+EP VEINING. NO SULPHIDES.
SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE. VOLCANIC BRECCIA WITH PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT (EP+CHL) OVERPRINTED BY CLAY ALTERATION. DOMINANT VEINING IS TYPE 3B (EP+PY), PARTIALLY DESTROYED BY CLAY ALTERATION.

SYN-VOLCANIC DIORITE INTRUSION. DISSEMINATED MAGNETITE AND SULPHIDES (CCP+PY). DOMINANT TYPE 2 VEINING. PROPYLITIC ALT OVERPRINTED BY CLAY ALTERATION. VEINS ARE PARTIALLY DESTROYED BY CLAY ALTERATION.

THIS UNIT STARTS WITH GOOD RQD ROCK AND GYPSUM VEINS. THIS WAS INTERPRETED TO BE A STRUCTURAL CONTACTS WITH A DIFFERENT UNIT. DARK MAGNETIC ANDESITE AND WHITE COLORED SILTSTONE OR RHYOLITE CLASTS. DOMINANT EP+CHL ALTERATION, MOSTLY VEIN CONTROLLED, WITH XENOLITHS PARTIALLY EP ALTERED. THIS WAS OVERPRINTED BY A WEAK SERICITE ALTERATION (MOSTLY IN THE GROUNDMASS). ABUNDANT GYPSUM AND TYPE 2 VEINS (EP+CAL). EP IS USUALLY AT THE SALVAGE OF GYPSUM VEINS SUGGESTING A REACTIVATION OF OLDER TYPE 2 VEINS. AT LEAST THREE SET OF GYPSUM VEINING CROSSCUTTING EACH OTHER. RARE EARLY MAGNETITE STRINGERS. DECENT BUT IRREGULAR CCP MINERALIZATION. MOSTLY OCCURING AS DUSTING IN THE XENOLITHS!, AND OCCASIONAL BLEBS. AT 284.15 M 3 CM THICK QTZ+CCP VEIN IS CUT BY LATER TYPE 2 VEINS IS PRESENT. ANOMOLOUS DIS CCP AROUND THIS VEIN. MOST OF THE CCP IS CONCENTRATED ON EP ALT.

ALTERED HEMATITE CLASTS ARE ALSO PRESENT. IN THE VEIN SECTION, FRACTURES FILLED WITH GYPSUM ARE OBSERVED, WHICH WERE LATER CUT BY FRACTURES FILLED WITH CALCITE. POLYMICTIC CONGLOMERATE WITH SUBROUNDED TO SUBANGULAR CLASTS (PREDOMINANT MINERALS SUCH CHLORITE AND SERICITE). PHYLLIC ALTERATION PREDOMINATE FROM 486.39M TO 492.2M. SOME ROUNDED EP ALTERED ROCK SEEN, ESPECIALLY NEAR THE BOTTOM OF INTERVAL, WHICH COULD BE THE REMNANTS OF VOLCANOCLASTIC CLASTS. SOME BROWN-RED GARNETS AT THE BOTTOM WHERE THERE IS LESS MAG,

MED GREEN AND GREY VOLCANOCLASTIC/BRECCIATED DIORITE. MED GRAINED CRYSTALS OF ALTERED/REPLACED PLAG AND HORNBLENDE VISIBLE. STILL A LOT OF SKARN ALT MASKING SOME TEXTURES. SUB-ANGULAR TO SUB-ROUND CLASTS THROUGHOUT, SOME COULD BE MANY CMS IN SIZE BUT BROKEN ROCK MAKES IT DIFFICULT TO TELL WHERE SOME CLASTS ARE. CLAST COMP LOOKS LIKE SOME DIORITES THAT ARE SOMETIMES THE SAME COLOUR OR LIGHTER, SOME DARKER ROCK THAT COULD BE MASSIVE MAG ALTERED ROCK, AND OCCASSIONALY PALE PINKISH BEIGE ROCK THAT COULD BE MORE RHYOLITIC. PATCHES OF WK-STR MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT. PATCHES OF BROWN-RED GARNETS IN SOME SPOTS THROUGHOUT.

PALE PURPLE AND DARK GREEN BRECCIATED RHYOLITE. THIS UNIT LOOKS TO BE RHYOLITIC IN COMPOSITION WITH CRYPTOCRYSTALLINE PALE PURPLE TO PINKISH ROCK THAT HAS BEEN FRAGMENTED BY ACT EP AND MAG RICH ROCK (SKARN FLUIDS?). CLASTS OF RHYOLITE ARE SUB-ANGULAR TO SUB-ROUND AND RANGE IN SIZE FROM A FEW MMS TO TENS OF CMS. THE BOTTOM OF UNIT HAS SLIGHTLY MORE SMALL ROUNDED CLASTS OF THE RHY AND SOME OF DIORITE FROM BELOW TOGETHER. ALTERATION CONSISTS OF MOSTLY HEM MAKING THE RHY SLIGHTLY DARKER PURPLE AND SKARN ALT IN THE ROCK FILLING THE FRACTURES. WK-STR PCHY MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT, MOSTLY WITHIN THE SKARN INFILL.

MED-DARK GREEN-BLACK VOLCANOCLASTIC DIORITE. MUCH LIKE THE DIORITE UNITS SEEN ABOVE WITH MANY VISIBLE SUB-ANGULAR TO SUB-ROUNDED CLASTS OF MULTIPLE LITHS THAT LOOK LIKE SOME LIGHT AND DARK DIORITES, ANDESITE, AND POSSIBLY RHYOLITE. UNIT IS QUITE BROKEN UP AND STRONGLY SKARN ALTERED BUT SOME TEXTURES AND CLASTS ARE STILL VISIBLE. LOTS OF EP, GRT, AND MAG THROUGHOUT.

DARK GREY-BLACK POSSIBLE HEALED FLT. SMALL INTERVAL IS MOSTLY QTZ AND PY VNING THAT COULD BE QSP VNING HEALING A FAULT AS IT IS INBETWEEN TWO DIFFERENT LOOKING UNITS. THERE ARE BANDS OF VEINING, BLACK SULPHIDES, QTZ, WHAT LOOKS LIKE DARK GREEN ACT, BLEBBY PY, AND EP THROUGHOUT INTERVAL. THE BANDING IS PROBABLY DUE TO SHEARING. SOME TR PATCHES OF MOD MAGNETISM.

LIGHT GREY-GREEN DIORITE. MED GRAINED, PHENOS OF ACICULAR HORNBLENDE AND PLAG THROUGHOUT. SKARN ALT HAS MOSTLY DISSAPEARED WITH NO MAG AND MUCH LESS EP (STILL PATCHY THROUGHOUT), SER ALT NOW DOMINATES. MINOR SMALL CLASTS SEE. STARTING AT 136.6M TO THE BOTTOM OF THE UNIT THERE LOOKS TO BE SHEARING ON AND OFF LEADING INTO THE NEXT UNIT. THERE IS ALSO ALSO A SMALL ANDESITE DYKLET FROM 140.57-140.77M. THIS ANDESITE DYKLET IS FINE GRAINED AND MED GREY, SLIGHTLY MAGNETIC, AND HAS AMYDULES FILLED WITH CAL. THIS DYKLET PROBABLY FORMED ALONG THE WEAK PLANE OF THE SHEARING/FLTING.

THERE IS POSSIBLY A THIRD ROCK TYPE IN IT AS ONE OR MORE OF THE FRAGMENTS LOOKS CLOSER TO A MINERALIZED ANDESITE OR DIORITE ROCK. ALTERATION LOOKS MOSTLY PROP EXCEPT THE MAG SKARN SPOTS.

VARIEGATED GREEN VOLCANICLASTIC ANDESITE, EXHIBITING A CHAOTIC ARRANGEMENT OF DIVERSE LITHIC CLASTS. THE MAJORITY OF THESE CLASTS ARE SUB-ROUNDED AND COULD POTENTIALLY CONTAIN VARIOUS COMPOSITIONS, SUCH AS ANDESITE, DIORITE, RHYOLITE, MONZONITE, AND POSSIBLY OTHERS. THE PRESENCE OF THESE DIFFERENT TYPES OF CLASTS GIVES THE ROCK A VISUALLY STRIKING AND VARIED APPEARANCE. SOME CLASTS ARE REPLACED BY PYRITE (PY) AND CHLORITE (CCP), WHILE ACTINOLITE IS REPLACED BY MAGNETITE. MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION, WITH ACTINOLITE/CHLORITE AND SERICITE, IS PRESENT IN THE GROUNDMASS OF THE ANDESITE AND IN SOME CLASTS. WEAK PERVASIVE HEMATIZATION IS EVIDENT IN SOME CLASTS. WEAK TO MODERATELY STRONG MAGNETITE ALTERATION IS OBSERVED IN PATCHES, POSSIBLY DUE TO THE PRESENCE OF MAGNETITE IN THE CLASTS. ADDITIONALLY, WEAK TO MODERATELY STRONG EPIDOTE ALTERATION IS SCATTERED IN PATCHES THROUGHOUT THE GROUNDMASS.

LIGHT GREY TO LIGHT GREEN, FG-MG, MODERATE PROP TO PHYLLIC ALT DIORITE. SMALL DYKES (<30 CM) OF ANDESITE ARE INTERBEDDED IN THE DIORITE. WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEAK PHYLLIC ALTERATION. CHLORITE AND EPIDOTE ARE DISTRIBUTED IN PERVASIVE FORM. MAGNETITE IS DISTRIBUTED LIKE PATCHY. CALCITE AND EPIDOTE VEINS ARE PREDOMINANT IN THIS INTERVAL. PY AND CCP ARE OBSERVED IN DISSEMINATED FORM.
MED GREY ANDESITE-MICRODIORITE DIKE FG. AMYGDULES OF CALCITE THROUGHOUT. WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. CALCITE VEINS ARE PREDOMINANT IN THIS INTERVAL.

LIGHT GREY TO LIGHT GREEN DIORITE. 913.42 -914.96M WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY THE MODERATE-STRONG POTASSIC ALTERATION. VARIEGATED GREY-GREEN POLYMICTIC VOLCANICLASTIC ANDESITE, EXHIBITING A CHAOTIC ARRANGEMENT OF DIVERSE LITHIC CLASTS. THE MAJORITY OF THESE CLASTS ARE SUB-ROUNDED AND COULD POTENTIALLY CONTAIN VARIOUS COMPOSITIONS, SUCH AS ANDESITE, DIORITE, RHYOLITE, MONZONITE, AND POSSIBLY OTHERS. (919.53-931.87M) WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINTED BY MODERATE PHYLLIC ALTERATION. ALSO, THE INTERVAL SHOWS MODERATE-STRONG SILICIFICATION. (931.87-935.31M) WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. SERICITE AND MAGNETITE ALTERATION ARE OBSERVED AS REPLACEMENT MINERALS INSIDE THE CLASTS. (935.31-947M) WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINTED BY MODERATE PHYLLIC ALTERATION. ALSO, THE INTERVAL SHOWS MODERATE-STRONG SILICIFICATION. CALCITE VEINS ARE PREDOMINANT IN THIS INTERVAL. QZ>CHL>PY>CCP VEINS WERE OBSERVED. PY WAS SEEN IN THE FORM OF BLEBS, AND CCP WAS DISSEMINATED.
MED-DARK GREY ANDESITE DIKE FG. WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEAK-MODERATE PERVASIVE MAGNETITE ALTERATION.

VARIEGATED GREY-GREEN POLYMICTIC VOLCANICLASTIC ANDESITE, EXHIBITING A CHAOTIC ARRANGEMENT OF DIVERSE LITHIC CLASTS. THE MAGNETITE IS OBSERVED IN THE FORM OF PATCHES. PYRITE IS OBSERVED IN BLEB FORMS. THE PRESENCE OF PYRITE INCREASES IN PHYLLIC ALTERATION. HOLD COMPLETED 1031M EOH.
CASING 9

LIGHT GREY-GREEN PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE. FINE-MED GRAINED, PHENOS OF ACICULAR HORNBLENDE, AUGITE AND PLAG THROUGHOUT. THE INTERVAL SHOWS ALTERNATION BETWEEN ANDESITE AND SUB-SYN-VOLCANIC MICRODIORITE INTRUSIONS. THE TRANSITIONS BETWEEN ANDESITE AND SUB-SYN-VOLCANIC MICRODIORITE INTRUSIONS ARE GRADUAL AND DIFFICULT TO DIFFERENTIATE. THE PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE IS PREDOMINANT IN THE INTERVAL. MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEAK PHYLLIC ALTERATION. EPIDOTE>CHLORITE>MAGNETITE. THE MAGNETITE PERVASIVE ALTERATION IS MOST COMMONLY OBSERVED IN ANDESITE FACIES. POTASSIC ALTERATION IS OBSERVED AS A HALO AROUND SOME VEINS. CALCITE VEINS PREDOMINATE IN THIS INTERVAL. VEINS QZ>PY>CCP ARE OBSERVED. AT A DEPTH OF 101M, VEIN WITH PROPYLITIC MINERALS, PYRITE (PY), CHALCOPYRITE (CCP) AND MOLYBDENUM (MO) IS OBSERVED. THIS VEIN ALSO EXHIBIT A POTASSIC ALTERATION HALO. IN THE INTERVAL FROM 94.38 TO 98 METERS, CLASTS WITH EPIDOTE AND POTASSIC ALTERATION ARE OBSERVED. ADDITIONALLY, THESE CLASTS EXHIBIT PY>CCP MINERALIZATION. PYRITE AND CCP ARE OBSERVED IN BLEB AND DISSEMINATED FORMS.

LIGHT GREY-GREEN PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE. FINE-MED GRAINED, PHEONS OF ACICULAR HORNBLENDE, AUGITE AND PLAG THROUGHOUT. THE INTERVAL RANGING FROM 179M TO 200M IS CHARACTERIZED BY MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION, WHICH HAS BEEN OVERPRINTED BY WEAK TO MODERATE POTASSIC ALTERATION. AT A DEPTH OF 184.05M, A VEIN WITH THE SEQUENCE QTZ>PY>GARNET IS OBSERVED. POTASSIC ALTERATION IS OBSERVED AS A HALO AROUND SOME VEINS. THE INTERVAL RANGING FROM 200M TO 225.47M IS CHARACTERIZED BY WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. CHLORITE AND EPIDOTE ARE DISTRIBUTED IN PERVASIVE FORM. GARNETS ARE PRESENT IN SOME QUARTZ (QTZ) VEINS ALONG WITH LATE-STAGE CALCITE. THE INTERVAL RANGING FROM 225.47M TO 233.7M IS CHARACTERIZED BY MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION, WHICH HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEEK-MODERATE POTASSIC ALTERATION. POTASSIC HALO ALTERATION ORIGINATES FROM A QUARTZ (QTZ) VEIN AT A DEPTH OF 230.80M-231.13M METERS. IN THIS VEIN GARNETS ARE OBSERVED. AFTER THE POTASSIC HALO, A PHYLLIC WEAK ALTERATION HALO IS OBSERVED. THE INTERVAL RANGING FROM 233.7M TO 357.39M IS CHARACTERIZED BY MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. MAGNETITE IS OBSERVED IN A PERVASIVE FORM. THE SEQUENCE OF MINERAL ASSEMBLAGE IS EP > CHL > MAGNETITE. A POTASSIUM ALTERATION HALO IS OBSERVED IN MOST VEINS. THE INTERVAL RANGING FROM 357.39M TO 366.22M IS CHARACTERIZED BY STRONG PROPYLITIC ALTERATION, WHICH HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEEK-MODERATE PHYLLIC ALTERATION. EP>CHL>SER>MAGNETITE>CLAY THE INTERVAL RANGING FROM 366.22M TO 410M IS CHARACTERIZED BY MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION, WHICH HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEEK-MODERATE SERICITE. EP>CHL>SER>MAGNETITE. THE INTERVAL RANGING FROM 410M TO 427.56M IS CHARACTERIZED BY WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION, WHICH HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEEK-MODERATE PHYLLIC ALTERATION. PHYLLIC ALTERATION IS OVERPRINTED BY EPITHERMAL ALTERATION. EP>CHL>SER>MAGNETITE. (INTERVAL 427.56-449.91) WEAK TO MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. CHLORITE AND EPIDOTE ARE DISTRIBUTED IN A PERVASIVE FORM. ADDITIONALLY, EPIDOTE IS PRESENT LIKE A VEINS (INTERVAL 449.91-455) STRONG PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEAK-MODERATE PHYLLIC ALTERATION. A POTASSIUM ALTERATION HALO IS OBSERVED IN MOST VEINS. (INTERVAL 455-486.47) WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEAK PHYLLIC ALTERATION. A POTASSIUM ALTERATION HALO IS OBSERVED IN MOST VEINS. CALCITE VEINS PREDOMINATE IN THIS INTERVAL. VEINS QTZ>PY> PROPYLITIC MINERALS>CCP ARE OBSERVED. AT A DEPTH OF 184.05 METERS, THE FIRST VEIN CONTAINING GARNET IS OBSERVED (SEQUENCE QTZ>PY>GARNET). THE PRESENCE OF VEINS WITH GARNET GRADUALLY INCREASES WITH DEPTH UP TO 427 METERS; AFTER THAT, THE PRESENCE OF THESE TYPES OF VEINS DECREASES. AT DEPTHS OF 278.30M, 287M, AND 297.06M, VEINS WITH QTZ> PROPYLITIC MINERALS> PYRITE (PY) >CHALCOPYRITE (CCP), AND MOLYBDENUM (MO) ARE OBSERVED. A POTASSIUM ALTERATION HALO IS OBSERVED IN MOST VEINS. THE PRESENCE OF VEINS WITH GARNET INCREASES GRADUALLY WITH THE DEPTH. THE DISTRIBUTION OF DISSEMINATED CCP AND PY BLEBS ARE RELATED TO EPIDOTE MINERALIZATION.
PINK-GREY MONZONITE DYKE. WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. VEINS QTZ>PY>CCP ARE OBSERVED. BLEBS PY IS OBSERVED THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL. A FEW DISSEMINATED CCP IS OBSERVED.

MEDIUM- DARK GREY-GREEN PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE WITH INTERCALATION OF MONZONITE DYKES (495-495.5 AND 500.70-501.23). (INTERVAL 488.73- 566) MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. CHLORITE-ACTINOLITE IS DISTRIBUTED IN A PERVASIVE FORM. WEAK TO MODERATE AMOUNTS OF EPIDOTE ARE OBSERVED, MOSTLY IN THE FORM OF VEINS. A POTASSIUM ALTERATION HALO IS OBSERVED IN MOST VEINS. (INTERVAL 566-607.45) WEAK-MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY MODERATE-STRONG PHYLLIC ALTERATIONS. CHLORITE-ACTINOLITE IS DISTRIBUTED IN A PERVASIVE FORM. WEAK TO MODERATE AMOUNTS OF EPIDOTE ARE OBSERVED, MOSTLY IN THE FORM OF VEINS. MAGNETITE AND CLAY ARE OBSERVED REPLACING MINERALS. CALCITE VEINS PREDOMINATE IN THIS INTERVAL. VEINS WITH QTZ>PY> CCP>MO ARE OBSERVED. BLEBS PY IS OBSERVED THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL. EP>ACT>PY>CCP VEINS USUALLY PRESENT A POTASSIUM HALO. A FEW DISSEMINATED CCP IS OBSERVED.
PINK-GREY MONZONITE DYKE. WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. VEINS QTZ>EP>PY>CCP ARE OBSERVED. BLEBS PY IS OBSERVED THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL. A FEW DISSEMINATED CCP IS OBSERVED.

MEDIUM- DARK GREY-GREEN PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE. MODERATE TO STRONG CHLORITE ALTERATION PREDOMINATES IN THE INTERVAL. CHLORITE HAS BEEN OVERPRINTED BY WEAK TO MODERATE SERICITIZATION AND MAGNETITE ALTERATION. CALCITE VEINS PREDOMINATE IN THIS INTERVAL. VEINS WITH QTZ>CAL> PY>CCP ARE OBSERVED. BLEBS PY IS OBSERVED THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL.
PINK-GREY MONZONITE DYKE. WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. VEINS WITH QTZ>PY> CCP ARE OBSERVED. BLEBS PY IS OBSERVED THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE DARK-GREY, GREY, MELANOCRATIC, FINE-TO-MEDIUM GRAINED, HORNBLENDE-PLAG (1-2MM) PHYRIC DIORITE. POOR PRESERVATION OF ORIGINAL TEXTURE DUE TO DEFORMATION AND ALTERATION. UNIT CONSISTS OF CONSISTENT PATCHES OF SHEARING ASSOCIATED WITH BLACK CHLORITE CLAY. LOCALIZED SECTIONS OF AUTO BRECCIATION. MODERATE-TO-STRONG PROPYLITIC ALTERATION DOMINATES, CONSISTING OF CHLORITE/ACTINOLITE-AFTER-GROUNDMASS/PHENOCRYSTS, PATCHES OF EPIDOTE BLEBS/EPIDOTE AND ENVELOPES AND WEAK-TO-MODERATE SERICITIZATION. PHENOCRYSTS (HORNBLENDE/PLAGIOCLASE) ARE OFTEN OBSERVED COMPLETELY REPLACED BY ACTINOLITE/CHLORITE ALONG WITH SERICITE-AFTER-FELDSPAR. INTERVAL ALSO HOSTS SECTIONS OF POTASSIC ALTERATION, OFTEN AS VEIN ENVELOPE. MAGNETITE IS DOMINATE THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL BOTH AS BLEBS REPLACING PHENOCRYSTS AND WITHIN FRACTURE IN FILL VEINS. THE UNIT IS MOSTLY DEVOID OF GROUNDMASS MINERALIZATION WITH ONLY TRACE DISSEMINATED/BLEB CHALCOPYRITE/PYRITE. MINERALIZATION IS MOST CONSISTENT WITHIN VEINS, ASSOCIATED WITH PROPYLITIC EPIDOTE VEINLETS AND WITHIN MAGNETITE FRACTURES/BLEBS. TRACE MOLYBDENITE OBSERVED WITHIN LOCALIZED SECTIONS OF EPIDOTE VEIN SELVAGE.

DARK GREY AND MED-DARK PINK BRECCIATED (POSSIBLY VOLCANOCLASTIC) MONZONITE. MED-DARK REDDISH PINK ROCK LOOKS TO BE MONZONITE, BRECCIATED (OR VOLCANOCLASTIC?) BY WHAT LOOKS LIKE FINE GRAINED ANDESITE. SOME POSSIBLE LAYERING IN THE ANDESITE OR FOLIATION FROM SHEARING, POSSIBLY SUB-VOLCANIC FLOWS. MUCH OF THE TEXTURE IN MONZONITE IS MASKED BY POT ALT, LOOKS TO BE MEDIUM GRAINED. CLASTS ARE SUB-ROUND TO ANGULAR WITH SMALLER CLASTS IN SOME PARTS, 0.1~5CM IN SIZE. SOME OF UNIT IS QUITE BROKEN UP AND BRITTLE, COULD BE POSSIBLE FAULTING. MOD MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT.

MED-DARK GREY SUBVOLCANIC/VOLCANOCLASTIC ANDESITE/DIORITE. MUCH OF ROCK SEEMS FINE GRAINED WITH MANY CLASTS OF DIORITE AND POSSIBLY MONZONITE AND ANDESITE. SOME OF GROUNDMASS SEEMS TO TRANSITION BETWEEN ANDESITE AND MICRODIORITE. SOME TEXTURES ARE MASKED BY ALT INCLUDING CLASTS, UP TO ~5CM. SOME SMALL HORNBLENDE PHENOS ~0.1CM IN SIZE SEEN IN ANDESITE. MOD MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT.

LIGHT RED TO LIGHT GREY INTRUSIVE GRANODIORITE. FROM THE START TO 834.4M THE UNIT IS MUCH REDDER FROM HEM STAINING AND SOME OF THE TEXTURE IS OBSCURED. AFTER THIS POINT THE GRAINSIZE IS COARSE WITH PLAG LATHS UP TO 0.8CM IN SIZE WITH ~15-20% HORNBLENDE UP TO ~0.4CM. SOME RARE MED GREY XENOLITHS SEEN ~3CM IN SIZE, POSSIBLE ANDESITE OR DIORITE LITH. WK-MOD MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT.
OVERBURDEN, BEDROCK HIT AT 9M? CASING TO 9M

MED-DARK GREY ANDESITE. MOSTLY FINE GRAINED, SOME THAT LOOKS LIKE MICRODIORITE, COULD BE PART OF THE SAME UNIT BELOW JUST WITH MORE ANDESITE PRESENT OR A TRANSITIONAL ZONE. POSSIBLY SUB-VOLCANIC OR TRANSITIONING TO ONE. SOME SUB-ANGULAR CLASTS OF EP ALTERED ROCK, ORIGINAL LITH DIFFICULT TO DISTINGUISH, POSSIBLE DIORITE/MICRODIORITE, UP TO 2CM IN SIZE. MOSTLY PROP ALT, SOME PATCHES OF PALER ROCK THAT COULD BE ARGILLIC. PCH WK-MOD MAGNETISM

MED-LIGHT GREY SUB-VOLCANIC MICRODIORITE TO DIORITE. MOSTLY VISIBLE MED GRAINS WITH SOME FINE SECTIONS, PLAG AND HORNBLENDE. ACT IS REPLACING MOST HORNBLENDE, SOME ACICULAR. SOME HAZY CLASTS ARE SLIGHTLY PALE AND SOMETIMES EP ALTERED, SUB-ANGULAR, UP TO 5CM. WK-MOD PCHY MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT.

LIGHT-MED GREY DIORITE. GRAINS ARE MORE VISIBLE IN THIS UNIT AND MOSTLY MED GRAINED. TOP LOOKS LIKE IT COULD BE TRANSITIONAL, MOST OBVIOUS CHANGE IS THE COLOUR AND ALTERATION FROM A MORE PROP ALTERED TO MORE SER/SIL AND POT SEEN. PLAG GRAINS ARE AROUND 2MM AND ACICULAR HORNBLENDE UP TO 3MM, NOT TOO MANY HORNBLENDE PHENOS SEEN COMPARED TO PLAG. SOME SMALL SECTIONS HAVE MINOR BRECCIATION, LOOKS LIKE MOSTLY PROP VNS ARE THE MAIN VN TYPE ASSOCIATED. POSSIBLE XENOLITHS, HARD TO TELL WITH POT AND SIL ALT MAKING TEXTURES HAZY. TR PCHY MAGNETISM.

PINK TO DARK GREY VOLCANOCLASTIC DIORITE. THIS UNIT IS COMPOSED OF CLASTS OF PROP ALTERED DARK GREY AND POT ALTERED PINK ROCK. THE PROP ALTERED ROCK LOOKS LIKE DIORITE WHILE THE POT ALTERED IS HARD TO SEE TEXTURE IN BUT PROBABLY SIMILAR TO THE UNIT ABOVE THAT WAS POT ALTERED AT THE BOTTOM. CLASTS ARE HARD TO DISTINGUISH PROPERLY AS THE PHYLLIC AND/OR ARGILLIC ALTERATION HAS MADE THE ROCK BRITTLE AND SOFT IN MANY SPOTS, BUT SOME VISIBLE ONES LOOK ~1-5CMS, POSSIBLY LARGER. THE UNIT LOOKS DIVIDED INTO SECTIONS AT TIMES WITH MORE DARK GREY PROP ALTERED ROCK FOR ABOUT A METRE WHICH TRANSITIONS TO POT ALTERED FOR ABOUT A METRE WITH EACH SECTION HAVING MINOR AMOUNTS OF THE OPPOSITE ALTERATION MATERIAL IN IT. THE UNIT ENDS WITH SOME RUBBLE MATERIAL AFTER 161M, COULD PROBABLY BE A FAULT/UNCONFORMITY.

PALE-MED GREY-GREEN DIORITE. ROCK IS ARGILLICALLY ALTERED AT TOP MAKING IT DIFFICULT TO DISTINGUISH TEXTURE EXCEPT NEAR SOME VEIN HALOS WHERE THE GRAINS ARE VISIBLE AND IN SOME PATCHES WHERE IT LOOKS LIKE DIORITE. THE UNIT BEGINS AFTER A RUBBLY SECTION THAT IS PROBABLY A FAULT/UNCONFORMITY. SOME SUB-ROUND TO SUB-ANGULAR CLASTS ARE VISIBLE, PROBABLE DIORITE COMP. SOME OF ROCK LOOKS FINER GRAINED LIKE A MICRODIORITE. THE UNIT BECOMES MORE PROP ALTERED FURTHER DOWN STILL WITH SOME PATCHES OF ARGILLIC ALT. THERE ARE WHAT LOOK LIKE A FEW SMALL POTASSIC DYKLETS AROUND 178.5-184M, UP TO 7CM WIDE. ALTERATION ALTERNATES BETWEEN PATCHY ARGILLIC AND PROP WITH SOME MINOR PHYLLIC AT THE BOTTOM.

MED GREY-GREEN BRECCIATED DIORITE. UNIT STARTS AFTER PHYL/SIL ALT ZONE AND HAS MANY PEBBLE-SIZED ANGULAR CLASTS WHICH DECREASE AND BECOME LARGER AFTER ABOUT A METRE. THE MAJORITY OF THE REST OF THE ROCK IS MATRIX SUPPORTED BRECCIA CLASTS UP TO POSSIBLY 10CMS. MATRIX IS FINE-MED GRAINED. CLASTS ARE SUB-ROUND TO SUB-ANGULAR, LOOK TO BE COMPOSED OF DIORITE AND POSSIBLY MONZONITE, SOME ARE HEAVILY ALTERED MAKING IT HARD TO DISTINGUISH. EP AND ARGILLIC LOOKING ALT COMMON IN CLASTS. MOD PCHY MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT

MED RED TO GREY MONZONITE. UNIT IS MOSTLY RED-PINK WITH MED GRAINED FELDSPAR AND SUB-ROUND TO ACICULAR HORNBLENDE WITH SOME SLIGHTLY GREY ZONES. PATCHES OF EP THROUGHOUT. MINOR CLASTS UP TO ~3CM IN SIZE, MONZONITE IN COMP, SUB-ANGULAR. RED-PINK ROCK IS POTASSIC, WHERE THERE IS LESS RED AND SLIGHTLY GREY THERE IS PROP ALT. WK-MOD MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT

MED GREY DIORITE BRECCIA. FINE-MED GRAINED DIORITE MATRIX WITH MANY SUB-ANGULAR TO SUB-ROUND CLASTS, MATRIX SUPPORTED. CLASTS COMPOSED OF DIORITE AND POSSIBLY OTHER LITHS, SOME TEXTURE DIFFICULT TO SEE DUE TO MOTTLED ALT. MOSTLY PROP AND PCHY ARGILLIC ALT WITH MINOR PHYL ALT.

LIGHT RED TO MED GREY MONZONITE. MED GRAINED, PLAG AND KSPAR WITH ~10-20% ACICULAR HORNBLENDE. LOOKS INTRUSIVE. THE START AND END DO NOT LOOK CHILLED AND THERE ARE CLASTS OF MONZONITE IN THE SURROUNDING ANDESITE/DIORITE BRECCIA SO THIS UNIT IS MOST LIKELY OLDER. ALTERATION IS MOSTLY POT KSPAR WITH PATCHES OF PROP ALT. WK-MOD MAGNETISM THROUGHOUT.

LIGHT-MED GREY ANDESITE BRECCIA. MANY CM SCALE CLASTS IN A MATRIX SUPPORTED ANDESITE. CLASTS ARE OBSCURED BY SLIGHTLY ARGILLIC ALT, LOOK TO BE COMPOSED OF MED GRAINED DIORITE AND POSSIBLY MONZONITE LIKE THE SURROUNDING UNITS. THERE ARE MANY PINK KSPAR RICH VNS THAT COULD BE FLUID LEAKING FROM THE ADJACENT MONZONITES. ANDESITE LOOKS TO HAVE PHENOS OF HORNBLENDE AND POSSIBLY PLAG. NON-MAGNETIC.

MED-DARK GREY ANDESITE BRECCIA. MOSTLY FINE GRAINED ANDESITE MATRIX WITH CLASTS OF MONZ AND/OR DIO AND ANDESITE FLOATING IN THE MATRIX. SAME MZN LITH AS PREVIOUS.

POSSIBLE ORDER OF EVENTS: INTRUSIVE MZN BODY FORMS, WEATHERING+EROSION TAKES PLACE, AND LITH FLOWS AROUND MZN OUTCROPS WHILE ALSO BREAKING OFF AND INCORPORATING CLASTS OF MZN, TILTING OF MZN + AND LITHS CREATING VERTICALLY ALTERNATING LITHS. BUT WHY IS MZN POT ALTERED WHEN AND LITH LARGELY ISNT? DID MZN ENABLE POT VEIN DIFFUSION? MZN-AND LITH CONTACTS ARE OFTEN SHARP + UNDULATING W/ NO EVIDENCE OF FAULTING. DATING OF MZN+AND LITHS WILL TELL US MORE ABOUT LITH RELATIONSHIPS.
AND FLOW RICH IN MZN CLASTS (MZN CLASTS FORMING >80% OF THIS SECTION). CONTRARY TO PREVIOUS MZN-CLAST-RICH SECTIONS, CCP DUSTING+SULFIDE VEINS REMAIN HIGH. LOCAL DECREASE IN MAG ALT IN MZN CLASTS.
REPEAT OF AND LITH. CONTAINS 5-10CM ANGULAR MZN CLASTS DECREASING IN OCCURRENCE WITH DEPTH.

PROTO-VOLCANICLASTIC/AUTOCLASTIC ANDESITE (SAME AND GRDMSS/MATRIX AS PREVIOUS LITH) WITH 2-5CM ANGULAR AUTOCLASTS OF MED-LIGHT GREEN MEDIUM-GRAINED PORPHYRYTIC ANDESITE THAT HAVE COMPLETE EPIDOTE REPLC OF GRDMSS + ACT REPLC OF HBL PHENO + MG RPLC OF MINOR VFG PX. LITTLE TO NO 1-3CM MZN CLASTS. LITH IS 90% AND GRDMSS/MATRIX + 10% AUTOCLASTS.

CHAOTIC POORLY-SORTED VOLCANICLASTIC ANDESITE (SAME AND AS PREVIOUS LITH) WITH ANGULAR 0.5-7CM CLASTS OF MED-LIGHT GREEN MED-GRN PORPHYRYTIC GRDMSS-RICH ANDESITE W/ SUBHEDRAL HBL PHENO + 5-30CM CLASTS MED-DARK MED-GRN PORPHYRITIC PHENO-RICH AND W/ ANHEDRAL HBL PHENO + 5-30CM OF APHANITIC LIGHT TO MED-GREY TUFF

LIGHT MOTTLED GREY-LIME APHANITIC PYROCLASTIC LAPILLI TUFF W/ WELDED STRUCTURES + ENTRAINED 2-50CM FRAGMENTED CLASTS OF BEDDED ORANGE-CREAM-BLACK MUDSTONE W/ 0.5CM BEDS AND 1-6CM CLASTS OF GREY-GREEN FG PORPHYRYTIC ANDESITE W/ ANHEDRAL PLAG OR HBL PHENO. SOME MUDSTONE CLASTS APPEAR TO HAVE UNDERGONE CONTACT METAMORPHISM / HORNFEL FACIES.
GREY MED-GRAINED PORPHYRITIC DIORITE DYKE W/ SUBHEDRAL ZONED PLAG PHENOS AND RARE HBL PHENOS REPLC BY ACT.

PYROCLASTIC LAPILLI TUFFITE, TUFFACEOUS LAYERS WITH RHYOLITE.MINERALIZATION (PY-CCP) IS ASSOCIATED WITH THE OCCURRENCE OF PROPYLITIC MINERALS AND MAGNETITE WITHIN CERTAIN LAYERS. AT A DEPTH OF 812 METERS, LAYERS EXHIBITING MODERATE TO INTENSE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION BECOME MORE PREVALENT. THESE LAYERS ALSO FEATURE A HIGH SULFUR CONTENT. THE SELECTIVE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION IN CERTAIN LAYERS COULD BE ATTRIBUTED TO THE GRAIN SIZE (POROSITY AND PERMEABILITY) OF THE DEPOSIT?.

GREY-GREEN FINE-GRAINED PORPHYRYTIC ANDESITE. MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINTED BY WEAK TO MODERATE SILICIFICATION. MAGNETITE AND SERICITE ARE OBSERVED AS SELECTIVE REPLACEMENT MINERALS. POTASSIC ALTERATION IS OBSERVED AS VEIN ENVELOPES. THE EPIDOTE VEINS ARE PREDOMINANT IN THE INTERVAL. SOME OF THESE VEINS HAVE A POTASSIC HALO. PY AND CCP ARE OBSERVED IN DISSEMINATED FORM.

THE FINE-GRAINED BRECCIATED DIORITE EXHIBITS AN OVERALL GRAY-GREEN APPEARANCE DUE TO THE PRESENCE OF PL AND AMPH IN ITS MINERAL COMPOSITION. MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINTED BY WEAK TO MODERATE SILICIFICATION. MAGNETITE AND SERICITE ARE OBSERVED AS SELECTIVE REPLACEMENT MINERALS. POTASSIC ALTERATION IS OBSERVED AS VEIN ENVELOPES. THE EPIDOTE VEINS ARE PREDOMINANT IN THE INTERVAL. SOME OF THESE VEINS HAVE A POTASSIC HALO. PY AND CCP ARE OBSERVED IN DISSEMINATED FORM.
PYROCLASTIC TUFFACEOUS LAYERS WITH RHYOLITE. MINERALIZATION (PY-CCP) IS ASSOCIATED WITH THE OCCURRENCE OF PROPYLITIC MINERALS AND MAGNETITE.

PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE DISPLAYS A GREEN-BLACK COLORATION AND PRESENTS A FINE TO MEDIUM GRAIN TEXTURE. THE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION IS EVIDENT WITH SECONDARY MINERALS LIKE CHLORITE AND SERICITE REPLACING THE PRIMARY CONSTITUENTS. FURTHERMORE, SOME OF THE EPIDOTE VEINS WITHIN THE ROCK CONTAIN PYRITE AND EXHIBIT A POTASSIC ALTERATION HALO.

GREY-GREEN BRECCIATED DIORITE. IT PREDOMINANTLY CONSISTS OF FINE-GRAINED DIORITE CLASTS SUSPENDED WITHIN AN ANDESITE/MICRODIORITE MATRIX. THE CLASTS VARY IN SIZE, REACHING APPROXIMATELY 1-2 CENTIMETERS, AND EXHIBIT SUB-ANGULAR TO SUB-ROUNDED SHAPES. THE EDGES OF MANY CLASTS ARE CHALLENGING TO DISCERN CLEARLY, AND A SIGNIFICANT NUMBER OF THEM DISPLAY SIGNS OF EPIDOTE ALTERATION OR FEATURE PATCHES OF EPIDOTE ALTERATION IN THEIR VICINITY.
GREY- BLACK XENOLITHIC RHYOLITE-BEARING ANDESITIC DYKE. MODERATE TO STRONG PROPYLITIC ALTERATION HAS BEEN OVERPRINT BY WEAK SILICIFICATION.
THE SAME PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE THAT IN PREVIOS INTERVALS.
GREY-BLACK, FINE-GRAINED ANDESITE DYKE WITH A HIGH BIOTITE CONTENT.
THE SAME PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE THAT IN PREVIOS INTERVALS.
GREY-BLACK, FINE-GRAINED ANDESITE DYKE WITH A HIGH BIOTITE CONTENT. WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION DISTRIBUTED PERVASIVELY. THE INTERVAL SHOWS MODETARTE SERICITE ALTERATION.
THE SAME PORPHYRITIC ANDESITE THAT IN PREVIOS INTERVALS. MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION WITH PERVASIVE DISTRIBUTION. SERICITE IS REPLACING SOME PHENOCRYSTALS.

GREY-GREEN BRECCIATED DIORITE. IT PREDOMINANTLY CONSISTS OF FINE-GRAINED DIORITE CLASTS SUSPENDED WITHIN AN ANDESITE/MICRODIORITE MATRIX. WEAK PROPYLITIC ALTERATION. EPIDOTE IS DISTRIBUTED AS VEINS IN THE INTERVAL. EOH

EXTREME RUBBLE AND CLAY, VERY LOW RECOVERY. DOMINANT LITHOLOGY IS PORPHYRY ANDESITE WITH SMALLER SECTIONS OF RHYOLITE, MONZONITE, AND BRECCIA. LOGGED AS OVERBURDEN BUT SOME PARTS ARE ANDESITIC BED ROCK. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALTERATION CHARACTERIZED WITH EP+CHL. DOMINANT ALT IS FRACTURE AND PERVASIVE FEOX. EP+CHL+CAL+/-PY VEINS AND LATE HEMATITE/CALCITE FRACTURES. 1.5% PY VEIN HOSTED. 0.5 DIS PY.
TRICONED DUE TO BAD GROUND. CASING ADVANCED.

PORPHYRY ANDESITE, SURFACE OXIDATION COUNTINUES DOWN TO 100 M. VERY LOW RECOVERY WITH CLAY AND RUBBLE SECTIONS. SLICKENLINES ARE COMMON. DOMINANT AND PRIMARY CHL WITH MINOR EP ALT (PROPYLITIC). PERVASIVE CHL, VEIN HOSTED EP. CLAY/SERICITE AND FEOX OVERPRINT. WEAK POTASSIC ENVELOPE AROUND QTZ VEINS. LATE CALCITE, QTZ+/-PY VEINS AND EP+/-PY VEINS.

PORPHYRY ANDESITE. SLIGHTLY DIFFERENT ALTERATION COMPARED TO THE UPHOLE UNIT (DRASTIC CHANGE IN EP CONTENT). COULD BE A DIFFERENT LITHOLOGY. STARTS AFTER FAULT. DOMINANT AND PRIMARY SERICITE+CHLORITE+CLAY ALT. WEAK POTASSIC ALT (K-FELD) AND PHYLLIC ENVELOPE AROUND QTZ VEINS. LATE CALCITE WITH STOCKWORK TEXTURE AT PARTS. QTZ+/-PY+/-MO VEINS WITH WEAK POT/PHYLLIC ENVELOPE. RARE TYPE 2 VEINS (EP+CAL). 1% DIS PY. 0.5% PY AND 0.05% MO IN QUARTZ VEINS. MO IS USUALLY IN THE SELVAGE OF QTZ VEINS.

POST-MINERAL DYKE DRASTICALLY DIFFERENT ALT/MIN COMPARED TO THE COUNTRY ROCK. LESS ALTERED. NO SULPHIDES AND NO MORE QTZ+PY+MO VEINS. STARTS AS A VERY FINE GRAINED ANDESITE/BASALT WITH QUARTZ AMYGDULES AND TRANSITS INTO MICRODIORITE NEAR THE CORE. SIMILARLY, DOWNHOLE CONTACT GRADES BACK INTO FINE GRAINED AMYGDULE ANDESITE. AMYGDULES ARE MOSTLY FILLED WITH QUARTZ AND MINOR EP OR K-FELDSPAR. DOMINANT ALT IS PHYLLIC (SER+QTZ). THIS WAS OVERPRINTED BY VERY WEAK PROPYLITIC ALT (TRACE EP). DOMINANT EP+CAL VEINS AND MINOR CONCANTRATED K-FELDPSAR VEINS.

POPRHYRY ANDESITE THAT LOOKS SIMILAR TO 112-123 M SECTION BUT LESS SCC ALTERED (ESPECIALLY CLAY). SOME INTERVALS GRADE INTO MICRODIORITE BUT A PORPHYRY ANDESITE COVERS MOST OF THE SECTION. DOMINANT AND PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT CHARACTERIZED WITH PERVASIVE ACT+CHL AND VEIN HOSTED EP. THIS WAS OVERPRINTED BY PHYLLIC ALT. PERVASIVE SER+QTZ AND WEAK PHYLLIC (SER+QTZ+PY) AROUND QTZ VEINS. DOMINANT QTZ+PY+/-MO+/-CPY VEINS, EP+PY VEINS AND LATE CALCITE VEINS. MOSTLY VEIN RELATED PY MINERALIZATION. 1% DIS PY AROUND THE VEINS, 1.5% HOSTED IN EITHER EP OR QTZ VEINS. QTZ+PY+CPY+MO VEINS. 0.05% MO, 0.2% CPY

HORNBLENDE DIORITE STARTS WITH A SHARP CONTACT. PRIMARY CHL ALT OVERPRINTED BY WEAK PHYLLIC (PY+SER/WHITE MICA) AND POT ALT. POT ALT CONSISTS OF K-FELD AND EP USUALLY AROUND THE ENVELOPE OF QTZ+PY+MO+/-CPY AND EP+CAL+PY VEINS. QTZ VEINS ARE CUT BY EP VEINS. PY REPLACING AMPHIBOLES AND WHITE MICA REPLACEMENT OF PLAGIOCLASE PHENOCRYSTS. 5% DIS PY, 0.5% VEIN HOSTED PY, 0.1% VEIN HOSTED MO, TRACE CPY IN VEINS.
ALTERED CONTACT AT BOTH ENDS (NOT ON RHYOLITE). QTZ+PY+MO VEINS, EP+PY+CAL AND CAL VEINS WITH SERICITIC ALT ENVELOPE. 1% DIS PY, 0.1% VEIN HOSTED PY IN QTZ AND EP VEINS. TRACE MO IN QTZ+PY+MO VEINS.

PORPHYRY ANDESITE THAT RESEMBLES 136.55-151.6 M SECTION. STARTS WITH A MORE DIORITIC TEXTURE BUT GRADES INTO A TRUE PORPHYRY ANDESITE AFTER 172 M. ORIGINAL PROPYLITIC (CHL+ACT+EP) OVERPRINTED BY SCC AND HEM ALT ENVELOPE AROUND CAL+SER VEINS. DOMINANT CAL+SER+HEM VEINS, MINOR QTZ+PY AND EP+ PY VEINS. 1.5% DIS PY.

AUGITE PHYRIC DIORITE STARTED WITH STRONG POTASSIC ALTERATION. LARGE AUGITE PHENOCRYSTS FLOATING ON SMALLER PLAGIOCLASE+AMPHIBOLE RICH GROUNDMASS (SECONDARY AUGITE DEVELOPMENT?). A FEW SECTIONS HAVE MONOMICT BX SECTION OF SIMILAR COUNTRY ROCK AND ACT+CHL RICH GROUNDMASS. THE FIRST 5 METER OF THIS UNIT HAS STRONG POTASSIC ALT (K-FELD+EP) CONCENTRATED AROUND EP+CAL+/-PY VEINS. AFTER THE POT ALT SECTION PHYLLIC BECOMES DOMINANT CHARACTERIZED WITH PERVASIVE QT, SER AND PY REPLACEMENT OF MAINLY AMPHIBOLES. PRIMARY ALT IS CHL+ACT OF AMPHIBOLES. 5% DIS PY, 0.5% VEIN HOSTED PY. RARE QTZ+MO+PY VEINS.

SIMILAR TO THE 158.61-165.27 M RHYOLITE DYKE. TOP CONTACT IS ALTERED, BOTTOM IS FAULTED. VERY FINE GRAINED. DOMINANT ALT IS PHYLLIC THAT SHOWS UP AROUND QTZ+PY AND CAL MICROFRACTURES (MOSTLY BLEACHED LOOK DUE TO SERICITE). EP+PY+CAL VEINS. 5% DIS PY.

SIMILAR DIORITE OBSERVED AT 180.42-195.28 M. DOMINANT AND PRIMARY PROPLYLITIC ALT CONSISTING OF CHL+ACT REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES, EP PATCHES AND EP+CHL VEINS. RARE QTZ+MO+PY VEINS. AFTER 222 M MASSIVE QTZ+PY+MO VEINS SHOW UP. K-FELDSPAR+EP ALT ENVELOPE AROUND THESE VEINS. ALBITIZATION ALSO STARTS AFTER 222 M AROUND CALCITE+ALBITE MICROFRACTURES AND OCCASIONALLY AROUND EP+CAL VEINS. THROUGHOUT UNIT 5% DIS PY REPLACING AMPHIBOLES. TRACE MO AND AROUND 0.1% PY IN QTZ+MO+PY VEINS.

POST-MINERAL BASALTIC-ANDESITIC DYKE WITH EPIDOTE FILLED AMYGDULES. VERY FINE GRAINED BROWN-GREEN GROUNDMASS. POSSIBLE PRIMARY BIOTITE? DOMINANT AND PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT (CHL+EP). THIS SECTION INCLUDES PARTS OF THE COUNTRY ROCK DIORITE BETWEEN 20-40 CM COUNTRY ROCK SECTIONS). TRACE PY. COUNTRY ROCK BETWEEN 228.88-229.12 M, 229.47-229.87 M, 235.1-235.48 M, 236.1-236.22 M. ALTERATION, VEINING AND MIN FOR THE COUNTRY ROCK IS SIMILAR TO THE UPHOLE/DOWNHOLE SECTIONS.
NOT SURE IF THIS IS THE SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE. THERE IS A DRASTIC CHANGE IN PY CONTENT! ALSO IT IS WAY MORE ALTERED AND FINE GRAINED. PROPER LITHO DESCRIPTION BETWEEN 243.3-251.57 M.

RHYOLITE DYKE SIMILAR TO THE OTHERS EXCEPT THE EP FILLED AMYGDULES. CUT BY MAFIC DYKE BETWEEN 238.6-239.16 M. DOMINANT PHYLLIC ALT (SER+QTZ VEIN ENVELOPE, PERVASIVE PY DUSTING) WITH MINOR PROPYLITIC (EP+CHL FILLED AMYGDULES) AND POT ALT AROUND QTZ VEINS. QTZ+PY+MO VEINS WITH TRACE CPY. 3% DIS PY.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-AUGITE PHYRIC ANDESITE/MICRODIORITE. LOOSELY PACKED PHENOS FLOATING ON A CHLORITIZED GROUNDMASS. PORPHYRITIC AND SERIATE PHYRIC. DOMINANT PHYLLIC ALT (SER+CHL+/-QTZ+/-CLAY) OVERPRINTED BY MODERATE POTASSIC ALT. QTZ+PY+MO VEINS WITH SER+QTZ ENVELOPE. RARE EP+PY VEINS. AFTER 248 M, NEAR THE RHYOLITE CONTACT, VEIN ALTERATION ENVELOPE BECOMES MORE POTASSIC (K-FELD+EP). 1% DIS PY, 0.3% VEIN HOSTED PY.
SIMILAR TO THE RHYOLITE DYKE AT 238.33-243.33 M. BROKEN WITH FAULT SLICKENLINES.

AUGITE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC ANDESITE. BROKEN, RUBBLEY AND INCLUDES CLAY SECTIONS. FEOX IS PRESENT. DOMINANT PROPYLITIC ALT CHARACTERIZED WITH ACT+CHL REPLACEMENT OF GROUNDMASS AND AMPHIBOLE PHENOCRYSTS. PHYLLIC OVERPRINT CONSISTING OF SERICITE AND QUARTZ. EP+PY VEINS. QTZ+/-PY VEINS. LATE CALCITE+HEMATITE VEINS. 0.5% DIS PY, 0.8% VEIN HOSTED PY.

ALTERED PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC ANDESITE STARTS AFTER A RUBBLED SECTION. FEOX STILL PRESENT. STRONG SCC ALT ENHANCED MOST OF THE ORIGINAL TEXTURE. SCC IS DOMINATED BY CLAY>CHLORITE>SERICITE. ORIGINAL ALT IS PROBABLY STILL PROPYLITIC. ALTERATION TRANSITS INTO MORE PHYLLIC (SER+QTZ) DOWNHOLE. CHL+PY VEINS, QTZ+PY VEINS. FAULT GOUGE BETWEEN 98-104 M.
CASING ADVANCED.
SAME AS 80-104 M SECTION.
CASING ADVANCED, CLAY AND SAND SEAM.

ESSENTIALLY THE SAME UNIT OBSERVED BETWEEN 80-104 M. LARGER PORPHYRITIC PLAGIOCLASE PHENOCRYSTS. GRADES INTO MICRODIORITE AT PARTS. CUT BY A 30 CM LATE DYKE AT 125.45 M (SIMILAR TO THE DOWNHOLE DYKE). LESS ALTERED WITH PRIMARY AND DOMINANT ALT BEING PROPYLITIC (ACT+CHL+/-EP). OVERPRINTED BY PHYLLIC (SER+QTZ). TRACE K-FELD ALT ENVELOPE AROUND QTZ VEINS. NO MORE FEOX. EP+CAL VEINS WITH EP+HEM ALT ENVELOPE. QTZ VEINS WITH COARSE GRAINED PY. SECTION ENDS WITH A CHILLED DYKE CONTACT.

POST MINERAL ANDESITIC DYKE. INCLUDES XENOLITHS OF THE COUNTRY ROCK. LESS ALTERED. NO SULPHIDES AND NO MORE QTZ+PY VEINS. STARTS AS A VERY FINE GRAINED ANDESITE/BASALT WITH QUARTZ AMYGDULES AND TRANSITS INTO MICRODIORITE NEAR THE CORE. SIMILARLY, DOWNHOLE CONTACT GRADES BACK INTO FINE GRAINED AMYGDULE ANDESITE. AMYGDULES ARE MOSTLY FILLED WITH QUARTZ AND MINOR EP. DOMINANT ALT IS PHYLLIC (SER+QTZ). THIS WAS OVERPRINTED BY VERY WEAK PROPYLITIC ALT (TRACE EP). DOMINANT LATE CAL AND RARE EP VEINS.
SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE. 123-141.15 M.
POST-MINERAL ANDESITE DYKE SIMILAR TO THE 141.15-174.52 M.
SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE. 123-141.15 M.
POST-MINERAL ANDESITE DYKE SIMILAR TO THE 141.15-174.52 M.
SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE. 123-141.15 M.

ANDESITE TRANSITS INTO A HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE. SAME ALTERATION AND VEINING STYLE AS THE ANDESITE. SECTION ENDS WITH AN EPIDOTE+PHYLLIC ALTERED RHYOLITE CONTACT. DOMINANT AND PRIMARY PROPYLITIC (ACT+EP+CHL REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES). PHYLLIC OVERPRINT (SER+PY+QTZ). CAL+EP+PY VEINS WITH EP+K-FELD ALT ENVELOPE. QTZ+PY+MO VEINS. 3% DIS PY.

SYN-MINERAL ANDESITE/DACITE? DYKE. STARTS AS FELDSPAR PORPHYRY AND GRADES INTO VERY FINE GRAINED. DOMINANT ALT IS PHYLLIC THAT SHOWS UP AROUND QTZ+PY AND CAL MICROFRACTURES (MOSTLY BLEACHED LOOK DUE TO SERICITE). EP+PY+CAL VEINS. 5% DIS PY.

AUGITE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE STARTS WITH A PHYLLIC ALTERED CONTACT. LARGE AUGITE PHENOCRYSTS FLOATING ON SMALLER PLAGIOCLASE+AMPHIBOLE RICH GROUNDMASS (SECONDARY AUGITE DEVELOPMENT?). SECTION BETWEEN 207-215 M IS CHARACTERIZED WITH A POLYMICT MAGMATIC BRECCIA WITH MILLED COUNTRY ROCK AND PINKISH-REDDISH MONZONITIC ROCK FRAGMENTS. AT 245.5 M A SMALL SYENITIC DYKE CUTS THE UNIT. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT (ACT+CHL REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES AND CHL+EP VEINS) OVERPRINTED BY PERVASIVE PHYLLIC ALTERATION (QTZ+SER+PY). AFTER 215 M EP+CAL+PY VEINS HAVE EP+HEM+ALBITE ALT ENVELOPE. POTASSIC? DOMINANT QTZ VEINING WITH THICKNESS RANGING FROM A COUPLE OF MM TO 30 CM. THESE VEINS USUALLY HOST PY AND SMALL AMOUNTS OF MO AND CPY. EP+CAL+PY+/-CHL VEINING. 5% DIS PY, 0.5% VEIN HOSTED PY. TRACE CPY AND MO.

PORPHYRY ANDESITE/DACITE? VERY FINE GRAINED GROUNDMASS WITH LARGER PLAGIOCLASE PHENOCRYSTS APPEARING AROUND LATE CALCITE/ALBITE VEINS (ALBITIZATION) AND CHLORITIZED AMPHIBOLES (AUGITE). THE UPHOLE DIORITE SEEMS TO BE TRANSITIONING INTO THIS UNIT RATHER THAN A SHARP CONTACT. ALTERATION AND VEINING IS RATHER SIMILAR. QTZ+PY+MO VEINS AND EP+CAL+PY VEINS WITH EP+K-FELD+PHYLLIC ALT ENVELOPE. ALT HALO STARTS WITH EP>K-FELD>PHYLLIC. ALBITIZATION ALONG LATE CALCITE/ALBITE VEINS. SILICIFICATION AND DIS PY ON THE GROUNDMASS.
MAFIC DYKE SIMILAR TO THE OTHERS. CUT BY AN ANGLE OF 40. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT (EP+CHL) OVERPRINTED BY SERICITE. NO SULPHIDES.

THIS SECTION IS A COMBINATION OF MAFIC DYKES INTRUDING DACITE/ANDESITE. INTERVALS ARE AS FOLLOWS: 258.13-259.91 M DACITE, 259.91-264.76 M DYKE, 264.76-266.92 M DACITE, 266.92-267.13 M DYKE, 267.13-268.7 M DACITE, 268.7-268.88 M DYKE, 268.88-273.02 M DACITE. AT THE END OF THIS SECTION (VERY BROKEN) LITHOLOGY CHANGES INTO DIORITE. ALTERATION AND VEINING HAS BEEN DESCRIBED IN PREVIOUS SECTIONS FOR EACH DIFFERENT LITHOLOGY.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE DIORITE. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC OP BY PERVASIVE PHYLLIC (SER+PY). CHL+/-ACT WITH MINOR EP REPLACING HORNBLENDE. EP+CAL VEINING. WEAK HEM DUSTING ON PLAG. CM- THICK SYENITIC DYKELETS. DOMINANT EP VEINING. QTZ VEINING BECOMES DOMINANT AFTER 281 M. QTZ WITH MINOR AMOUNTS OF PY AND TRACE MO/CPY.
MAFIC DYKE JUST AS BEFORE.

SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE. PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE DIORITE. BRECCIA TEXTURE WITH POTASSIC ALT (PATCHES OF K-FELD+EP) IS VERY COMMON. GENERALLY MONOMICT IN NATURE. TECTONIZED. BRECCIATED QUARTZ VEINS AND STOCKWORK OF CALCITE FRACTURES (ESPECIALLY AFTER 320 M). CORE OF THE SYSTEM? PRIMARY PROPYLITIC OVERPRINTED BY PERVASIVE PHYLLIC (SER+PY). CHL+/-ACT WITH MINOR EP REPLACING HORNBLENDE. MOST OF THE EP+CAL AND QTZ+PY VEINS HAVE PHYLLIC ALT ENVELOPE. PATCHY POTASSIC ALT (K-FELD+EP) USUALLY TOGETHER WITH QTZ VEINS WITH AN OUTER EDGE BEING WEAKLY ALBITIZED. WEAK PERVASIVE CLAY ALT IN BROKEN/GOUGE ZONES. DOMINANT QTZ+/-K-FELD+PY+MO+/-CPY VEINING. EP+CAL+/-CHL VEINING. SMALL AMOUNTS OF W HAS BEEN CONFIRMED WITH XRF AND UNDER UV LIGHT IN A COUPLE OF EP VEINS. CUT BY MAFIC LATE DYKE BETWEEN 314.84-315.45 M.

PLAGIOCLASE PHYRIC DACITE/ANDESITE DYKE INTRUDING THE COUNTRY ROCK DIORITE. 416.57-417.2 M DACITE, 417.2-418.33 M DIORITE, 418.33-424.46 M DACITE, 424.46-425.26 M DIORITE, 425.26-425.83 M DACITE, 425.83-428.77 M DIORITE, 428.77-438.17 M DACITE, 438.17-441.64 M DIORITE, 441.64-443.56 M DACITE. USUALLY AT THE DYKE CONTACTS COUNTRY ROCK HAS CHILLED MARGINS (HORNFELSED). ESSENTIALLY BOTH LITHOLOGIES ALTERED AND MINERALIZED SIMILARLY. SYNVOLCANIC DYKES. PRIMARY AND DOMINANT PROPYLITIC ALT (CHL+EP REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES). ABUNDANT EPIDOTE VEINS AND EP ALT PATCHES. PHYLLIC ALT IS STRONGER IN DACITE DYKES AND SHOWING UP AROUND THE VEIN ENVELOPE OF EP VEINS. DIS PY AND PERVASIVE SERICITE. QTZ+PY+MO VEINS AND EP+CAL VEINS USUALLY HAVE POTASSIC ALT ENVELOPE (K-FELD+EP). CALCITE STOCKWORK.

AFTER THE DYKE UNIT BECOMES MORE BRECCIATED AND POLYMICTIC. STARTS AS AN INTRUSIVE BRECCIA BETWEEN 497.07-452 M AND GRADES INTO VOLCANIC BRECCIA AFTER 452 M. HORNBLENDE-AUGITE SERIATE PHYRIC. XENOLITHS ARE MOSTLY POT+EP ALTERED BUT THEY ARE POLYMICT IN NATURE (DIFFERENT TEXTURED DIORITE AND MONZODIORITE CLASTS). DOMINANT AND PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT OVERPRINTED BY PHYLLIC AND POTASSIC ALT. CHL+ACT REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES. EP VEINS AND STRONG EP+K-FELD PATCHES. EP ALTERED XENOLITHS. K-FELDSPAR ENVELOPE AROUND EP VEINS. PERVASIVE SER+QTZ. SILICIFIED. QTZ VEINING INTENSITY DROPS. GYPSUM VEINING SHOWS UP AROUND 451 M. 0.5% DIS PY, 0.1% VEIN HOSTED PY (MAINLY EP VEINS).

PLAGIOCLASE-AUGITE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE WITH 80-90% PLAGIOCLASE. THIS UNIT LOOKS SIMILAR TO THE DACITE/ANDESITE DYKES BUT MORE INTRUSIVE WITH WELL DEFINED PHENOCRYSTS.
