TRICONE AND ADVANCING CASING

THIS UNIT HAS BEEN LOGGED AS ANDESITE IN OTHER HOLES ON THIS PAD. IT IS THE SAME UNIT BUT IN THIS HOLE IT LOOKS MORE INTRUSIVE. PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-AUGITE PHYRIC DIORITE/MICRODIORITE. BROKEN, RUBBLY AND INCLUDES CLAY SECTIONS UP TO 117.62 M. FEOX IN FRACTURES, CLAY GOUGE, PYRITE BEARING VEINS (UP TO 117.62 M). PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT (ACT+CHL REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES, EP-VEINS) OVERPRINTED BY VEIN ENVELOPE POT ALTERATION AND PERVASIVE PHYLLIC ALT (QTZ AND WHITE MICA DOMINATED). LATE HEM+/-CAL VEIN. QTZ+MO+PY VEINS WITH THICKNESS RANGING FROM MM UP TO 3 CM. 40 TCA. <10% SULPHIDES IN TOTAL WITH PY BEING DOMINANT. OCCASIONALLY HAS POT ALT ENVELOPE (K-FELD+EP) OR K-FELD WITHIN THE VEINS. MINOR PY VEINS AND BARREN EP+CAL VEINS.

SAME UNIT OUT OF FAULT ZONE AND FEOX ALT. INCREASE IN POT ALT DUE TO AN INCREASE IN K-FELD+/-QTZ+/-EP+/-PY VEINS. QTZ+MO+PY+/-CCP VEINS WITH THICKNESS RANGING FROM MM UP TO 3 CM. 40 TCA. <10% SULPHIDES IN TOTAL WITH PY BEING DOMINANT.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-AUGITE PORPHYRY ANDESITE BRECCIA. LOOSELY PACKED PLAGIOCLASE AND ACT+CHL ALTERED AMPHIBOLES FLOATING ON A SILICIFIED GROUNDMASS (STRONG QUARTZ PRESENCE). XENOLITHS OF DIFFERENT SOURCE (MOSTLY INTRUSIVE) THAT ARE PARTIALLY OR COMPLETELY REPLACED BY EPIDOTE WITH A LATER DIS PY/CCP OVERPRINT. EP VEINS AND EP+/-POT ALT HALOS AROUND VEINS. DIS MAGNETITE ON AMPHIBOLES. WEAK POT ALT WITH EP USUALLY AROUND SULPHIDE BEARING VEINS. PERVASIVE PHYLLIC ALT WITH A STRONG QUARTZ COMPONENT REPLACING THE GROUNDMASS. MINOR SER AND PY. SOME SECTIONS WITH CLAY FAULT GOUGE. STRONG FEOX ON FRACTURES AND PY-BEARING VEINS. STRONGLY BROKEN, CLAY ALTERED, RUBBLY. EP ONLY VEINS. QTZ+PY+/-MO VEINS. EP+PY+/-CCP VEINS. POT+EP ALT ENVELOPE AROUND QTZ AND EP+PY+CCP VEINS. QTZ VEINS ARE LESS FREQUENT BUT THICKER (CM-SCALE) WITH AN ANGLE OF 75 TCA. EP VEINS ARE MORE CHAOTIC WITH ~30 TCA. <10% SULPHIDES IN QTZ VEINS. 20-30% SULPHIDES ON EP VEINS. PY>CCP. 2.5% DIS PY USUALLY SELECTIVE ON EP ALT PATCHES OR EP ALTERED XENOLITHS. SOME ON ALTERED AMPHIBOLES. MINOR DIS CCP ON EP ALT (0.1%). TRACE MALACHITE ON FEOX ALTERED FRACTURES OBSERVED AT ~30 M. TRACE MO ON FRACTURES AND QTZ VEINS. EP+PY+CCP VEINS WITH TOTAL 0.5% PY AND 0.05% CCP. QTZ HOSTED PY 0.1%, TRACE CCP AND TRACE MO. DIS MAG ON AMPHIBOLES (3%).
CASING ADVANCED. BAD GROUND. TRICONED.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-AUGITE PORPHYRY ANDESITE BRECCIA. LOOSELY PACKED PLAGIOCLASE AND ACT+CHL ALTERED AMPHIBOLES FLOATING ON A SILICIFIED GROUNDMASS (STRONG QUARTZ PRESENCE). XENOLITHS OF DIFFERENT SOURCE (MOSTLY INTRUSIVE) THAT ARE PARTIALLY OR COMPLETELY REPLACED BY EPIDOTE WITH A LATER DIS PY/CCP OVERPRINT. EP VEINS AND EP+/-POT ALT HALOS AROUND VEINS. DIS MAGNETITE ON AMPHIBOLES. WEAK POT ALT WITH EP USUALLY AROUND SULPHIDE BEARING VEINS. PERVASIVE PHYLLIC ALT WITH A STRONG QUARTZ COMPONENT REPLACING THE GROUNDMASS. MINOR SER AND PY. SOME SECTIONS WITH CLAY FAULT GOUGE. STRONG FEOX ON FRACTURES AND PY-BEARING VEINS. STRONGLY BROKEN, CLAY ALTERED, RUBBLY. EP ONLY VEINS. QTZ+PY+/-MO VEINS. EP+PY+/-CCP VEINS. POT+EP ALT ENVELOPE AROUND QTZ AND EP+PY+CCP VEINS. QTZ VEINS ARE LESS FREQUENT BUT THICKER (CM-SCALE) WITH AN ANGLE OF 75 TCA. EP VEINS ARE MORE CHAOTIC WITH ~30 TCA. <10% SULPHIDES IN QTZ VEINS. 20-30% SULPHIDES ON EP VEINS. PY>CCP. 2.5% DIS PY USUALLY SELECTIVE ON EP ALT PATCHES OR EP ALTERED XENOLITHS. SOME ON ALTERED AMPHIBOLES. MINOR DIS CCP ON EP ALT (0.1%). MALACHITE ON FEOX ALTERED FRACTURES OBSERVED BETWEEN 24-38 M. TRACE MO ON FRACTURES AND QTZ VEINS. EP+PY+CCP VEINS WITH TOTAL 0.5% PY AND 0.05% CCP. QTZ HOSTED PY 0.1%, TRACE CCP AND TRACE MO. DIS MAG ON AMPHIBOLES (3%).
CASING ADVANCED. BAD GROUND. TRICONED.

SYN-MINERAL/VOLCANIC GRANODIORITE DYKE. SYN-MINERAL BECAUSE OF THE QTZ+MO VEINS. >20% QUARTZ, PLAGIOCLASE>K-FELD. PRIMARY BIOTITE BOOKS AND HORNBLENDE PHENOCRYSTS. CHL+SER+/-EPIDOTE ALTERED HORNBLENDES AND BIOTITE. PHYLLIC OVERPRINT (MOSTLY SERICITE WITH SOME QUARTZ AND PY, HARD TO DISTINGUISH SECONDARY QUARTZ FROM PRIMARY). BAD GROUND WITH RUBBLE, EXTREMELY BROKEN AND FAULT GOUGE SECTIONS. HEMATITE IN FRACTURES AND HEM DUSTING OF GROUNDMASS. 2% DIS PY ON ALTERED HORNBLENDES. MO IN FRACTURES. <1 CM QTZ+MO+PY VEINS SOMETIMES WITH EP AND POT ALT ENVELOPE.

ESSENTIALLY THE SAME UNIT BEFORE THE DYKE BUT SLIGHTLY DIFFERENT LITHOLOGY. LOOSELY SPACED AMPHIBOLE AND PLAGIOCLASE PHENOCRYSTS FLOATING ON A QUARTZ RICH GROUNDMASS (QUARTZ IS INTERPRETED TO BE SECONDARY DUE TO PHYLLIC ALT). NOT A TRUE ANDESITE, NOT A TRUE DIORITE. NOT A VOLCANIC BRECCIA ANYMORE, PORPHYRY SUB-VOLCANIC ANDESITE. STILL, IN TERMS OF ALTERATION, TIMING AND VEIN/MIN STYLE THE SAME UNIT AT THE TOP. PERVASIVE REPLACEMENT OF EP+ACT+CHL ON AMPHIBOLES. EP AND CHL VEINS. FEOX ALT ON FRACTURES AND PY-BEARING VEINS. QUARTZ RICH GROUNDMASS THAT WAS INTERPRETED AS PART OF THE PHYLLIC ALT RATHER THAN PRIMARY. PY AND MINOR SER. PERVASIVE MAGNETITE. EP+/-PY+/-CHL VEINS. NOT ALL OF THEM HAVE SULPHIDES, MOST OF THEM ARE JUST EP. 2.5% EP ONLY, 0.5% PY-ONLY, 0.5% EP+PY+CHL. PARTIALLY OXIDIZED IN -PY BEARING VEINS (FEOX). 1% QTZ+K-FELD+PY+MO VEINS WITH TRACE CCP. 1.5% DIS PY, MINOR DIS CCP ON EP ALT PATCHES. DIS MAG.

LITHOLOGY GRADUALLY SWITCHED TO MORE DIORITIC APPEARANCE. STILL NOT A TRUE DIORITE, NOT A TRUE ANDESITE. SOMETHING IN BETWEEN. MICRODIORITE AT SOME SECTIONS. SUBVOLCANIC INTRUSION. CONTINUATION OF THE ANDESITE UNIT ABOVE WITH DIFFERENT LITH. DOMINANT PROPYLITIC ALT WITH ACT+CHL REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES, EP PATCHES, EP ALT ENVELOPE ALONG QTZ VEINS, AND EP VEINS. PERVASIVE MAGNETITE REPLACING AMPHIBOLES. WEAK POT ALT ENVELOPE ALONG VEINS. QTZ VEINS WITH K-FELDSPAR. PERVASIVE PHYLLIC ALT WITH DOMINANT QTZ+PY WITH MINOR SERICITE. QTZ+K-FELD+PY VEINS WITH MINOR MO. POT/EP ALT ENVELOPE. BETWEEN 0.5-2 CM THICKNESS AND 50 TCA. 20 TO 30% PY. <10% MO. EP ONLY VEINS, EP+PY VEINS. RARE PY ONLY VEINS. QTZ VEINS CUT EP VEINS. BETWEEN 2 TO 3% FINE TO COARSE GRAINED DIS PY. TRACE DIS CCP. TRACE MO IN QTZ VEINS. ~1% VEIN HOSTED PY SHARED AMONG QTZ VEINS, EP VEINS, AND RARE PY-ONLY VEINS.
GRANODIORITE LOOKS SIMILAR TO THE ONE ABOVE. MORE ALTERED. MAIN PROPYLITIC ALT OVERPRINTED BY POTASSIC AND WEAK PHYLLIC. QTZ+K-FELD+PY+/-MO VEINS CUTTING EP VEINS.

LITHOLOGY GRADUALLY SWITCHED TO MORE DIORITIC APPEARANCE. STILL NOT A TRUE DIORITE, NOT A TRUE ANDESITE. SOMETHING IN BETWEEN. MICRODIORITE AT SOME SECTIONS. SUBVOLCANIC INTRUSION. CONTINUATION OF THE ANDESITE UNIT ABOVE WITH DIFFERENT LITH. CHANGING BACK TO VOLCANIC BRECCIA AROUND 182 M. DOMINANT PROPYLITIC ALT WITH ACT+CHL REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES, EP PATCHES, EP ALT ENVELOPE ALONG QTZ VEINS, AND EP VEINS. PERVASIVE MAGNETITE REPLACING AMPHIBOLES. WEAK POT ALT ENVELOPE ALONG VEINS. QTZ VEINS WITH K-FELDSPAR. PERVASIVE PHYLLIC ALT WITH DOMINANT QTZ+PY WITH MINOR SERICITE. QTZ+K-FELD+PY VEINS WITH MINOR MO. POT/EP ALT ENVELOPE. BETWEEN 0.5-2 CM THICKNESS AND 50 TCA. 20 TO 30% PY. <10% MO. EP ONLY VEINS, EP+PY VEINS. RARE PY ONLY VEINS. QTZ VEINS CUT EP VEINS. BETWEEN 2 TO 3% FINE TO COARSE GRAINED DIS PY. TRACE DIS CCP. TRACE MO IN QTZ VEINS. ~1% VEIN HOSTED PY SHARED AMONG QTZ VEINS, EP VEINS, AND RARE PY-ONLY VEINS.
BEDROCK AT SURFACE.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-AUGITE PHYRIC QUENCHED DIORITE. LARGER AMPHIBOLE COMPARED TO PLAGIOCLASE PHENOCRYSTS. PLAGIOCLASE RICH GROUNDMASS. CHL ALT AMPHIBOLES OVERPRINTED BY WEAK TO MODERATE SERICITE+CLAY ALT. LATE CALCITE VEINS WITH HEMATITE. DIS PY.
CASING PUSHED TO 30 M.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-AUGITE PHYRIC QUENCHED DIORITE. LARGER AMPHIBOLE COMPARED TO PLAGIOCLASE PHENOCRYSTS. PLAGIOCLASE RICH GROUNDMASS. PARTIALLY TRANSITS INTO ANDESITE PORPHYRY. CHL ALT AMPHIBOLES OVERPRINTED BY MODERATE TO STRONG SERICITE+CLAY ALT. LATE CALCITE VEINS WITH HEMATITE. OCCASIONAL PATCHES WITH PINKISH COLORED ALT (POT OR HEM?). DIS PY AND CPY. PY>CPY. MINERALIZATION DESTRUCTIVE RETROGRADE ALT. POOR RECOVERY AND RQD THROUGHOUT THIS SECTION. FAULT GOUGE AND DARK SULPHIDE RICH CLAY IS PRESENT AT THE END OF THIS UNIT. 96-97 M MAGNETITE+/-SULPHIDE RICH RUBBLE/CLAY. THERE IS NO CLEAR DOWNHOLE CONTACT. SOMEWHERE IN THE CLAY LITHOLOGY CHANGES INTO POT ALTERED DIORITE-MONZODIORITE WITH GYPSUM VEINS.

MINERALIZED DIORITE STARTS WITH A FAULTED CONTACT. ORIGINAL ALT IS PROBABLY PROPLYITIC WITH AMPHIBOLES REPLACED BY CHL+ACT. PROPYLITIC VEINS GETTING REPLACED BY MAG+HEM. ORIGINAL ALT WAS OVERPRINTED BY POT ALT FOLLOWING VEIN ENVELOPE. OCCASIONAL SPOTS WITH SECONDARY BIOTITE. THIS HOLE UNIT WAS OVERPRINTED BY MAG+SULPHIDE DESTRUCTIVE RETROGRADE ALT. STRONG CLAY-SERICITE ALTERATION REPLACED MOST OF THE PHENOCRYSTS. INTENSITY OF RETROGRADE DESTRUCTIVE ALT DECREASES DOWNHOLE. XENOLITHS FROM THE DOWNHOLE UNIT STARTS TO SHOW UP AROUND 156 M. XENOLITHS UP TO 30 CM AND RARELY CCP MINERALIZED. XENOLITH COMPOSITION INCREASES AFTER 194 M CLOSER TO THE CONTACT. WELL DEVELOPED MONOMICT BRECCIA STARTS AROUND THE SAME DEPTH (181 M). USUALLY COMES WITH STRONG POT ALTERATION. DOMINANT LATE GYPSUM VEINING CUTTING OR CENTERLINING EARLIER VEINS. MOST OF THIS SECTION HAS VERY LITTLE DISSEMINATED SULPHIDES. 106.4-140 M CCP+/-MAG VEINS. 140-149 M BORNITE STARTS TO SHOW UP AS IN DARK COLORED MICA-BORNITE MACURALS/GHOST VEINS. OCCASIONAL CCP CLUSTERS WITHIN POT+HEM ALTERED PATCHES. UNIT ENDS WITH MAG+ACT MATRIX BRECCIA WITH MILLED CLASTS.

HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE WITH PLAGIOCLASE RICH GROUNDMASS. PORPHYRITIC. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT WITH AMPHIBOLES REPLACED WITH CHL AND PROPYLITIC VEINS (CHL+ACT+/-EP). STRONG VERY FINE PERVASIVE MAGNETITE ALTERATION. EARLY PROPYLITIC VEINS REPLACED WITH MAGNETITE. SOME OF THE MAG TURNED INTO HEMATITE. SERICITE OVERPRINT IS STILL STRONG REPLACING HORNBLENDE AND PLAGIOCLASE. MAGNETITE+HEMATITE VEINS WITH HEM ALT ENVELOPE. LATE GYPSUM VEINING CUTTING AND CENTERLINING EARLIER VEINS. NO DIS SULPHIDE. A COUPLE OF HYDROTHERMAL BRECCIA SECTIONS WITH POTASSIC ALT (MAG+K-FELD+BT) OVERPRINTED BY SER E.G. 231.5-235.61 M AND 272.88-278 M. CCP+BN IN THESE SECTIONS. HIGHEST GRADE ZONE IS 258.85-263.61 M WITHIN A CLAY-SERICITE RICH RETROGRADE ALT. BANDED BN+MAG STRINGERS. DIS BN ON MAG REPLACED MAFIC SITES. POT ALT CONCENTRATED AROUND BN-BEARING VEINS.BN MACULARS WITH POT ALT ENVELOPE. POSSIBLE CHALCOCITE TOGETHER WITH BN? THIS UNIT SEEMS TO END AFTER A LAST PULSE OF HYDROTHERMAL BRECCIA BETWEEN 272.88-278 M.

TRANSITION INTO PLAGIOCLASE+HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE. SERIATE PHYRIC BUT NOT PORPHYRITIC. RELATIVELY LESS POTASSIC ALTERED AND LESS GYPSUM VEINING. NOT SURE IF THIS IS A DIFFERENT INTRUSION BUT DEFINITELY LOWER GRADE AFTER THE CONTACT. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT WITH CHLORITE REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES AND MINOR EP. A COUPLE OF PROPYLITIC VEINS COMPOSED OF CHL+EP AND HEM ALT ENVELOPE. PROPYLITIC ALT OVERPRINTED BY PERVASIVE HEMATITE AND SERICITE ALT. HEMATITE DUSTED CALCITE AND GYPSUM VEINS ARE DOMINANT. MINOR SERICITE+CHLORITE VEINS. DISSEMINATED MAG/HEM ON MAFIC SITES. NO DISSEMINATED SULPHIDES. THIS UNIT IS CUT BY POST-MINERAL FELDSPAR PORPHYRY DYKES BETWEEN 370.26-374.27 M, 416.3-420.5 M AND 430.57-4XX M. THESE DYKES DON’T CARRY GYPSUM (POST-GYPSUM) AND SULPHIDES, BUT THEY TEND TO REMOBILIZE SULPHIDES IN THE HOST ROCK. CLOSER TO THE DYKES A COUPLE OF QTZ VEINS SHOW UP WITH CCP AND BN.

FELDSPAR PORPHYRY HEMATITE ALTERED POST-MINERAL DYKE. COARSER LOOSELY SPACED FELDSPAR AND FINER AMPHIBOLE PHENOCRYSTS (DOMINANT HORNBLENDE, MINOR AUGITE). SERIATE PHYRIC. PRIMARY CHL ALT OF AMPHIBOLES OVERPRINTED BY SERICITE ALTERATION. INTENSITY OF SERICITE ALT INCREASES TOWARDS THE EDGES. MASSIVE CALCITE VEINING PARALLEL TCA.

PLAGIOCLASE+HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE. SERIATE PHYRIC BUT NOT PORPHYRITIC. RELATIVELY LESS POTASSIC ALTERED AND LESS GYPSUM VEINING. NOT SURE IF THIS IS A DIFFERENT INTRUSION. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT WITH CHLORITE REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES AND MINOR EP. A COUPLE OF PROPYLITIC VEINS COMPOSED OF CHL+EP AND HEM ALT ENVELOPE. PROPYLITIC ALT OVERPRINTED BY PERVASIVE HEMATITE AND SERICITE ALT. HEMATITE DUSTED CALCITE AND GYPSUM VEINS ARE DOMINANT. MINOR SERICITE+CHLORITE VEINS. DISSEMINATED MAG/HEM ON MAFIC SITES. NO DISSEMINATED PY. THIS UNIT IS CUT BY POST-MINERAL FELDSPAR PORPHYRY DYKES BETWEEN 370.26-374.27 M, 416.3-420.5 M AND 430.57-455.61 M. THESE DYKES DON’T CARRY GYPSUM (POST-GYPSUM) AND SULPHIDES, BUT THEY TEND TO REMOBILIZE SULPHIDES IN THE HOST ROCK. CLOSER TO THE DYKES A COUPLE OF QTZ VEINS SHOW UP WITH CCP AND BN. MINERALIZATION BEGINS AFTER THE HYDROTHERMAL BRECCIA SECTION BETWEEN 491-560 M.

FELDSPAR PORPHYRY HEMATITE ALTERED POST-MINERAL DYKE. COARSER LOOSELY SPACED FELDSPAR AND FINER AMPHIBOLE PHENOCRYSTS (DOMINANT HORNBLENDE, MINOR AUGITE). SERIATE PHYRIC. PRIMARY CHL ALT OF AMPHIBOLES OVERPRINTED BY SERICITE AND HEMATITE ALTERATION. NO GYPSUM IN THE DYKE (POST-GYPSUM PHASE).

PLAGIOCLASE+HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE. SERIATE PHYRIC BUT NOT PORPHYRITIC. RELATIVELY LESS POTASSIC ALTERED AND LESS GYPSUM VEINING. NOT SURE IF THIS IS A DIFFERENT INTRUSION. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT WITH CHLORITE REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES AND MINOR EP. A COUPLE OF PROPYLITIC VEINS COMPOSED OF CHL+EP AND HEM ALT ENVELOPE. PROPYLITIC ALT OVERPRINTED BY PERVASIVE HEMATITE AND SERICITE ALT. HEMATITE DUSTED CALCITE AND GYPSUM VEINS ARE DOMINANT. MINOR SERICITE+CHLORITE VEINS. DISSEMINATED MAG/HEM ON MAFIC SITES. NO DISSEMINATED PY. THIS UNIT IS CUT BY POST-MINERAL FELDSPAR PORPHYRY DYKES BETWEEN 370.26-374.27 M, 416.3-420.5 M AND 430.57-455.61 M. THESE DYKES DON’T CARRY GYPSUM (POST-GYPSUM) AND SULPHIDES, BUT THEY TEND TO REMOBILIZE SULPHIDES IN THE HOST ROCK. CLOSER TO THE DYKES A COUPLE OF QTZ VEINS SHOW UP WITH CCP AND BN. MINERALIZATION BEGINS AFTER THE HYDROTHERMAL BRECCIA SECTION BETWEEN 491-560 M.

FELDSPAR PORPHYRY HEMATITE ALTERED POST-MINERAL DYKE. COARSER LOOSELY SPACED FELDSPAR AND FINER AMPHIBOLE PHENOCRYSTS (DOMINANT HORNBLENDE, MINOR AUGITE). SERIATE PHYRIC. PRIMARY CHL ALT OF AMPHIBOLES OVERPRINTED BY SERICITE AND HEMATITE ALTERATION. ARGILLIC (CLAY) ALT ENVELOPE AROUND ALBITE VEINS AT FAULTED SECTION BETWEEN 437.4-441 M. NO GYPSUM IN THE DYKE (POST-GYPSUM PHASE).

PLAGIOCLASE+HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE. SERIATE PHYRIC BUT NOT PORPHYRITIC. RELATIVELY LESS POTASSIC ALTERED AND LESS GYPSUM VEINING. NOT SURE IF THIS IS A DIFFERENT INTRUSION. PRIMARY PROPYLITIC ALT WITH CHLORITE REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES AND MINOR EP. A COUPLE OF PROPYLITIC VEINS COMPOSED OF CHL+EP AND HEM ALT ENVELOPE. PROPYLITIC ALT OVERPRINTED BY PERVASIVE HEMATITE AND SERICITE ALT. HEMATITE DUSTED CALCITE AND GYPSUM VEINS ARE DOMINANT. MINOR SERICITE+CHLORITE VEINS. DISSEMINATED MAG/HEM ON MAFIC SITES. NO DISSEMINATED PY. THIS UNIT IS CUT BY POST-MINERAL FELDSPAR PORPHYRY DYKES BETWEEN 370.26-374.27 M, 416.3-420.5 M AND 430.57-455.61 M. THESE DYKES DON’T CARRY GYPSUM (POST-GYPSUM) AND SULPHIDES, BUT THEY TEND TO REMOBILIZE SULPHIDES IN THE HOST ROCK. CLOSER TO THE DYKES A COUPLE OF QTZ VEINS SHOW UP WITH CCP AND BN. MINERALIZATION BEGINS AFTER THE HYDROTHERMAL BRECCIA SECTION BETWEEN 491-560 M.

THIS IS THE HYDROTHERMAL/MONOMICT BRECCIA SECTION OF THE SAME DIORITE INTRUSION. BRECCIA WITH 5 TO 10% SERICITIC+MAGNETITE/HEMATITE MATRIX AND POT ALTERED MONOMICT CLASTS. MINERALIZATION AND ALTERATION USUALLY RAMP UP CLOSER TO THE BRECCIATED SECTIONS. POT ALTERATION IS CONFINED MOSTLY AROUND VEINS OR BRECCIATED ZONES. SERICITE AND HEMATITE IS STILL DOMINANT. ORIGINAL PROPYLITIC ALT (ACT+CHL AND MINOR EP) OVERPRINTED BY SERICITE AND HEMATITE. PERVASIVE SERICITE. SERICITE GRADES INTO CLAY AT SOME INTERVALS (526-528 M). ANOMALOUS DIS MAG/HEM. HEM STRINGERS. QTZ+CCP VEINS WITH EP+POT ALT ENVELOPE. CCP BRECCIA VEIN WITH POT ALT ENVELOPE. BN+CCP DUSTING AROUND AND IN MAG/HEM STRINGERS. BANDED MAG+BN+ACT VEINS. BN+MAG CRACKLES. CCP AND BN BLEBS. DIS CCP AND BN. CAL+BN+CCP+CHL STOCKWORK VEINS. POSSIBLY CAL+CHL ARE LATE EVENTS THAT CUT AND CENTERLINE EARLIER CU-BEARING VEINS. SECTION ENDS WITH ALBITE+QTZ VEINING.

SECTION STARTS WITH A SERICITE RICH MATRIX SUPPORTED MONOMICT BRECCIATED CONTACT BETWEEN 598.35-600 M AND PASSES INTO INTENSE SERICITE+CLAY RICH ARGILLIC ALTERATION. MONOMICT BRECCIA IS ALSO PRESENT BETWEEN 624-631 M AND 684 M. THESE MONOMICT BRECCIATED SECTIONS OFTEN COMES WITH ARGILLIC/SERICITE ALTERATION AND HAVE DECENT MINERALIZATION. ORIGINAL TEXTURE IS ALMOST COMPLETELY ENHANCED DUE TO THE INTENSE ALTERATION BUT A COUPLE OF SPOTS SUGGESTS A PLAGIOCLASE-AUGITE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC ANDESITE. ARGILLIC ALTERATION PASSES INTO MORE SERICITE RICH RETROGRADE ALTERATION. ORIGINAL PROPYLITIC ALTERATION IS VISIBLE IN LESS ALTERED SECTIONS. CHL+ACT REPLACEMENT OF AMPHIBOLES, OVERPRINTED BY SERICITE. PROPYLITIC VEINS (CHL+EP) WITH HEMATITE ALT ENVELOPE. HIGHEST GRADE SECTION IS BETWEEN 635-661 M DEFINED WITH ARGILLIC ALTERED MONOMICT BRECCIA. ---- POSSIBLY THIS HOLE UNIT IS POTASSIC ALTERED BEFORE THE RETROGRADE ALTERATION. THERE ARE EVIDENCES OF SECONDARY BIOTITE AND FAINT RED COLORED ENVELOPE AROUND CU-BEARING VEINS WHICH ARE POSSIBLY K-FELDSPAR.---- A COUPLE OF QTZ+BN+CCP VEINS RUN PARALLEL TCA (637.07-638.09 M AND 653.6-659.41). NOTE: THE QUARTZ VEINS MENTIONED HERE ARE NOT PURE SIO2, BUT A MIXTURE OF QUARTZ AND CALCITE. POSSIBLY, THE CALCITE IS SECONDARY IN NATURE AND FRACTURE FILLING. ALBITE VEINING IS COMMON MOST LIKELY RELATED WITH ARGILLIC ALTERATION. DIS BN+CCP+MAG FOUND IN CLUSTERS WITH DARK COLORED MAFIC SITES (POSSIBLY RELICT ACTINOLITE).

AUTOBRECCIATED HORNBLENDE-PLAGIOCLASE DIORITE GREY-BLACK FINE-TO-MEDIUM GRAINED PLAGIOCLASE HORNBLENDE (1-2MM) DIORITE. ALTERATION CONSISTS PRIMARY OF PROPYLITIC ALTERATION CHLORITE/SERICITE AFTER GROUNDMASS/MATRIX AND GRAINS +/- TRACE EPIDOTE. OFTEN MAFIC PHENOCRYSTS SUCH AS HORNBLENDE ARE ENTIRELY REPLACED BY CHLORITE +/- PYRITE/CHALCOPYRITE (AND OCCASIONALLY MAGNETITE) GIVING THE ROCK A SPOTTED MACUOLE TEXTURE. PYRITE CONTINUES TO MAKE UP 1.5-3% OF THE UNIT WITH CHALCOPYRITE HOSTED BOTH WITHIN BLEBS/VEINS AND DISSEMINATED. THROUGHOUT THE UNIT LOCALIZED SECTIONS OF AUTOCLASTIC (DIORITE WALL ROCK CLASTS) BRECCIATION ARE OBSERVED. DIORITE CLASTS RANGE FROM 0.5-5CM IN SIZE AND OFTEN CONTRASTING WITH HOST ROCK IN ALTERATION. VEINING CONSISTS PRIMARILY OF (BUT NOT LIMITED TO), NONMINERALIZED TYPE V6 LATE CALCITE STRINGERS AND QSP VEINS. QSP VEINS VERY IN CALCITE CONTENT AND OFTEN HOST PYRITE AND CHALCOPYRITE WITHIN VEIN OR SELVAGE +/- SERICITE.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-DIORITE GREY-BLACK, MESOCRATIC, EQUIGRANULARITY, MEDIUM-GRAINED, (1-2MM) PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE DIORITE. SHORT INTERVAL OF DIORITE (POSSIBLY DYKE, OR RAFT), INTERVAL CONSISTS OF 2% PYRITE BY CONTENT, MOST OF WHICH OBSERVED REPLACING HORNBLENDE PHENOCRYSTS. FEW XENOLITHIC CLASTS OF PREVIOUS UNIT ANDESITE. UNIT IS DOMINATED BY PHYLLIC ALTERATION, WEAK SERICITE AFTER PLAGIOCLASE, CHLORITIZATION OF HORNBLENDE PHENOCRYSTS AND MODERATE SILICIFICATION. FEW PATCHES OF STRONG ARGILLIC ALTERATION PRESENT. OVERALL THE UNIT HAS FAR BETTER PRESERVATION OF ORIGINAL TEXTURE WHEN COMPARED TO NEIGHBOURING LITHOLOGICAL UNITS. SECTION COULD BE AN INTRUSIVE DYKE OR SIMPLY A SECTION OF DIORITE LESS AFFECTED BY ALTERATION. INTENSE ALTERATION ON INTERVAL CONTACTS IS INTERPRETED AS CHILLED MARGINS AND LIKELY A POST ALTERATION DYKE.

AUTOBRECCIATED HORNBLENDE-PLAGIOCLASE DIORITE MELANOCRATIC, GREY-BLACK, FINE-TO-MEDIUM GRAINED, EQUIGRANULAR, HORNBLENDE-PLAGIOCLASE DIORITE. LITHOLOGY CONTAINS INTERVALS OF AUTOCLASTIC BRECCIATION SUPPORTED BY A FINE-GRAINED DIORITE. DIORITE WALL ROCK CLASTS RANGE FORM 0.5-5CM IN DIAMETER AND ARE OFTEN OBSERVED WITH PYRITE NUCLEATING AROUND ITS BOUNDARIES (AS WELL AS DISSEMINATED WITHIN THE CLAST). LITHOLOGY IS PRIMARILY DOMINATED BY PHYLLIC ALTERATION CONSISTING OF MODERATE SILICIFICATION, SERICITE AND DISSEMINATED PYRITE. HOST ROCK IS SHOWN TO BE MORE PHYLICALLY ALTERED COMPARED TO ITS AUTOBRECCIA DIORITE CLASTS. MINERALIZATION CONSISTS OF PRIMARLY 1.5-3% DISSEMINATED PYRITE AND TRACE CHALCOPYRITE.

ANDESITE DYKE GREY-GREEN, MELANOCRATIC, APHANITIC, HORNBLENDE-PHYRIC ANDESITE, INTERPRETED AS A POST MINERAL DYKE. HORNBLENDE PHENOCRYSTS 1MM IN DIAMETER SUPPORTED IN A MEDIUM GREEN ANDESITE MATRIX. ALTERATION DOMINATED BY MODERATE PROPYLITIC ALTERATION CONSISTING OF FINE-GRAINED CHLORITE (+/-ACTINOLITE?) AFTER GROUNDMASS AND MAGNETITE AFTER MAFIC HORNBLENDE PHENOCRYSTS, +/- HEMATITE. DYKE DOES NOT HOST ANY SIGNIFICANT MINERALIZATION WITH NO VISIBLE PYRITE. VEINING CONSISTS SOLELY OF TYPE V6 LATE CALCITE STRINGERS +/-HAEMATITE STAINING.

ANDESITE CLASTS ARE OBSERVED TO HAVE ELEVATED LEVELS OF PYRITE DISSEMINATION (RELATIVE TO HOST ROCK). ALTERATION CONSIST OF A COMBINATION OF A BASE PROPYLITIC CHLORITE, SERICITE +/- EPIDOTE AFTER GRAINS AND AN OVERLYING PHYLLIC ALTERATION. PHYLLIC ALTERATION CHARACTERIZED BY MODERATE SILICIFICATION, SERICITE AND ELEVATED DISSEMINATED PYRITE. VEINING CONSIST ALMOST EXCLUSIVELY OF TYPE V6 LATE CALCITE STRINGERS +/- SERICITE AND +/- HEMATITE AND QSP VEINS +/-EPIDOTE, +/-CHALCOPYRITE AND MANY HOSTING POTASSIC/SERICITE ENVELOPES.

CHLORITE-MAGNETITE AFTER GROUNDMASS, SERICITIZATION OF PLAGIOCLASE PHENOCRYSTS AND POTASSIC ENVELOPES AMONGST QSP VEINS. TRACE-TO-0.5% DISSEMINATED PYRITE, TRACE DISSEMINATED CHACLOPYRITE AND 1% MAGNETITE BLEBS. VEINIG DOMINATED BY TYPE V6 LATE CALCITE STRINGERS, QSP VEINS HOSTING POTASSIC ENVELOPES AND PYIRTE-MAGNETITE FRACTURE INFILL STRINGERS.
CASING TO 28.55.

MAGNETITE IS OBSERVED TO BEGIN REPLACING HORNBLENDE/MAFIC PHENOCRYSTS AT 67.61-89.15M. ALTERATION IS DOMINATED BY SERICITE ALTERATION BREAKING DOWN PHENOCRYSTS AND GROUNDMASS INTO SERICITE/CLAY. SECTION IS COMPOSED ENTIRELY OF RUBBLE AND GOUGE, DIFFICULT TO ESTIMATE % ABUNDANCES.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE FAULTING AND QUENCHED ARGILLIC ALTERATION ALONG LITHOLOGICAL CONTACT. MOST MINERALIZATION IS HELD WITHIN MAGNETITE-PYRITE STRINGERS +/- CHALCOPYRITE WITH ONE EXCEPTION WHERE CHALCOPYRITE WITHIN CHLORITE MACULES ARE OBSERVED FROM 198.67M AND ONWARDS. THROUGHOUT THE INTERVAL GYPSUM/ANHYDRITE VEINS ARE DOMINATE AT 3-5% ABUNDANCE, SOME VEINS HOST CHALCOPYRITE/PYRITE SELVAGE BUT IS LIKELY MAG VEINS CENTERLINED BY THE POST MINERALIZED GYPSUM/ANHYDRITE VEINS. ALTERATION DOMINATED BY WEAK-TO-MODERATE SERICITE-PROPOLITIC ALTERATION CONSISTING OF SERICTE/CHLORIET( NO EPIDOTE)-AFTER GROIUBDMASS/PHENOCRSYST. AMONGST THE INETRVAL THERE ARE LOCALIZED PATCHES OF POTASSIC ALTERATION ORIGINATING FROM POST-MINERLAIZATION GYPSUM VEINS. IN SOME SECTIONS THE POTASSIC ENEVELOPS ARE OBSERVED WITH FINE-GRAINED DISSEMINATED BIOTITE, INTERPRETED AS RESTROGRADE METAMORPHISM BIOTITE-AFTER-EARLIER SERICIFICATION. BASED OFF THIS OBSERVATION, PROPOLITIC ALTEARTION WOULD BE THE EARLY PRIMARY ALTEATION WITH GYPSUM/ANHYDRITE + POTASSIC VEINS HAVING COME IN AFTERWARDS.

PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE PHYRIC DIORITE GREY-GREEN, MESOCRATIC, MEDIUM-GRAINED (1-3MM), PLAGIOCLASE-HORNBLENDE-+/-AUGITE, DIORITE. RELIC VEINS CONTINUE FROM PREVIOUS INTERVALS LEAVING BEHIND VUGGED REMNANT VEINS, SOME VUGS HAVE PRESERVED CHALCOPYRITE/PYRITE AND BORNITE SELVAGE. THE FIRST BORNITE IS OBSERVED AT 218.90M. WEAK POTASSIC ALTERATION RELATIVELY COMMON, HEAVILY ASSOCIATED WITH CONSISTENCY OF GYPSUM VEIN ENVELOPES. FEW PATCHES OF LOCALIZED ARGILLIC ALTERATION.
